Detailed Description

Represents a graph structure used for bucket-based optimization in a solver.

The BucketGraph class provides various functionalities for managing and optimizing a graph structure using buckets. It includes methods for initialization, configuration printing, node visitation checks, statistics printing, bucket assignment, and more.

The class also supports parallel arc generation, label management, and feasibility checks. It is designed to work with different directions (Forward and Backward) and stages of the optimization process.

Note
This class relies on several preprocessor directives (e.g., RIH, RCC, SRC) to conditionally enable or disable certain features.
Template Parameters
DirectionThe direction of the graph (Forward or Backward).
StageThe stage of the optimization process.
FullIndicates whether the full labeling algorithm should be used.
ArcTypeThe type of arc (Bucket or regular).
MutabilityIndicates whether the label is mutable or immutable.

BucketGraph::BucketGraph ( const std::vector< VRPNode > & nodes,
int horizon,
int bucket_interval,
int capacity,
int capacity_interval )

Constructs a BucketGraph object.

This constructor initializes a BucketGraph with the given nodes, time horizon, and bucket interval. It sets up the forward and backward buckets, initializes the dual values for the CVRP separation, and defines the intervals and resource limits.

◆ assign_buckets() [1/2]

template<Direction D>
auto & BucketGraph::assign_buckets ( auto & FW,
auto & BW )
inlineconstexprnoexcept

Assigns the buckets based on the specified direction.

This function returns a reference to the buckets based on the specified direction. If the direction is Forward, it returns a reference to the forward buckets. If the direction is Backward, it returns a reference to the backward buckets.

Template Parameters
DThe direction (Forward or Backward).
Parameters
FWThe forward buckets.
BWThe backward buckets.
Returns
A reference to the buckets based on the specified direction.

int eta1,
int eta2,
int eta_max,
int nC )

Augments the memories in the BucketGraph.

This function takes a solution vector, a SparseModel, and several parameters to augment the memories in the BucketGraph. It identifies cycles in the SparseModel that meet certain conditions and forbids them in the BucketGraph. The function prioritizes smaller cycles and limits the number of forbidden cycles based on the given parameters.

Definition at line 341 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

343 {
344 // Pre-allocate an estimated number of cycles (assume about 25% of paths
345 // have cycles)
346 std::vector<std::vector<uint16_t>> cycles;
347 cycles.reserve(paths.size() / 4);
348
349 // Detect cycles in fractional paths
350 for (size_t pathIndex = 0; pathIndex < paths.size(); ++pathIndex) {
351 // Skip paths that are nearly integral.
352 const double epsilon = 1e-3;
353 if (solution[pathIndex] <= epsilon) continue;
354
355 const auto &path = paths[pathIndex];
356 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<uint16_t, int> visited_clients;
357 visited_clients.reserve(path.size());
358
359 // Iterate over the nodes in the path to detect cycles.
360 for (size_t i = 0; i < path.size(); ++i) {
361 uint16_t client = path[i];
362 // Skip depot nodes.
363 if (client == 0 || client == N_SIZE - 1) continue;
364
365 auto it = visited_clients.find(client);
366 if (it != visited_clients.end()) {
367 // Cycle found: extract the cycle from the first occurrence to
368 // the current index.
369 std::vector<uint16_t> cycle;
370 cycle.reserve(i - it->second + 1);
371 for (size_t j = static_cast<size_t>(it->second); j <= i; ++j) {
372 cycle.push_back(path[j]);
373 }
374 cycles.push_back(std::move(cycle));
375 break; // Process only one cycle per path.
376 }
377 visited_clients[client] = i;
378 }
379 }
380
381 if (cycles.empty()) return;
382
383 // Sort cycles by increasing size (small cycles are processed first)
384 pdqsort(cycles.begin(), cycles.end(),
385 [](const auto &a, const auto &b) { return a.size() < b.size(); });
386
387 int forbidden_count = 0;
388
389 // Lambda: count the number of set bits in the neighborhoods bitmap for a
390 // node.
391 auto count_neighborhood_bits = [this, eta_max](uint16_t node) -> int {
392 int count = 0;
393 // neighborhoods_bitmap[node] is assumed to be a vector of uint64_t
394 // segments.
395 for (const auto &segment : neighborhoods_bitmap[node]) {
396 count += __builtin_popcountll(segment);
397 if (count >= eta_max) return count;
398 }
399 return count;
400 };
401
402 // Process each detected cycle.
403 for (const auto &cycle : cycles) {
404 if (cycle.empty()) continue;
405
406 bool can_forbid = true;
407 // Check each node in the cycle to see if the neighborhood bit count is
408 // below eta_max.
409 for (uint16_t node : cycle) {
410 if (count_neighborhood_bits(node) >= eta_max) {
411 can_forbid = false;
412 break;
413 }
414 }
415
416 // If the cycle qualifies (small cycle or limited forbidden count),
417 // forbid it.
418 if (can_forbid && (cycle.size() <= static_cast<size_t>(eta1) ||
419 forbidden_count < eta2)) {
420 // Convert cycle from uint16_t to int vector (if needed by
421 // forbidCycle)
422 std::vector<int> int_cycle(cycle.begin(), cycle.end());
423 forbidCycle(int_cycle, false);
424 if (++forbidden_count >= eta2) break;
425 }
426 }
427}

template<Stage S, Symmetry SYM>
std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::bi_labeling_algorithm ( )

Performs the bi-labeling algorithm on the BucketGraph.

Definition at line 429 of file BucketSolve.h.

429 {
430 // === Stage-specific Initializations ===
431 if constexpr (S == Stage::Three) {
432 heuristic_fixing<S>();
433 } else if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
434 if (first_reset) {
435 reset_fixed();
436 first_reset = false;
437 }
438#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
439 if (options.bucket_fixing) {
440 bucket_fixing<S>();
441 }
442#endif
443 }
444
445 // Reset pools and perform common initialization.
446 reset_pool();
447 common_initialization();
448
449 // Pre-allocate c-bar vectors for forward and backward buckets.
450 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(fw_buckets.size());
451 std::vector<double> backward_cbar(bw_buckets.size());
452
453 // Run the labeling algorithms.
454 if constexpr (SYM == Symmetry::Asymmetric) {
455 run_labeling_algorithms<S, Full::Partial>(forward_cbar, backward_cbar);
456 } else {
457 forward_cbar =
458 labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, S, Full::Partial>();
459 }
460
461 // Acquire an initial best label from the forward label pool.
462 auto best_label = label_pool_fw->acquire();
463 if constexpr (SYM == Symmetry::Asymmetric) {
464 // Compute the best label if the forward and backward labels are
465 // feasible.
466 if (check_feasibility(fw_best_label, bw_best_label)) {
467 best_label = compute_label<S>(fw_best_label, bw_best_label);
468 } else {
469 best_label->cost = 0.0;
470 best_label->real_cost = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
471 best_label->nodes_covered.clear();
472 }
473 } else {
474 best_label->cost = best_label->real_cost;
475 best_label->real_cost = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
476 best_label->nodes_covered.clear();
477 }
478 merged_labels.push_back(best_label);
479
480 // Declare atomics to track the best cost and non-dominated label count.
481 std::atomic<double> best_cost{0.0};
482 std::atomic<size_t> non_dominated_labels_per_bucket{0};
483
484 // === Parallel Bucket Processing ===
485 // Choose a chunk size based on hardware concurrency.
486 const size_t chunk_size =
487 fw_buckets_size / std::thread::hardware_concurrency();
488 const size_t n_chunks = (fw_buckets_size + chunk_size - 1) / chunk_size;
489
490 auto bulk_sender = stdexec::bulk(
491 stdexec::just(), n_chunks,
492 [this, chunk_size, &best_cost,
493 &non_dominated_labels_per_bucket](std::size_t chunk_idx) {
494 const size_t start_bucket = chunk_idx * chunk_size;
495 const size_t end_bucket =
496 std::min(start_bucket + chunk_size,
497 static_cast<size_t>(fw_buckets_size));
498
499 // Process each bucket within the current chunk.
500 for (size_t bucket = start_bucket; bucket < end_bucket; ++bucket) {
501 // Process non-dominated labels in the current bucket.
502 const auto &bucket_labels = fw_buckets[bucket].get_labels();
503 for (const Label *L : bucket_labels) {
504 if (L->is_dominated) continue;
505
506 non_dominated_labels_per_bucket.fetch_add(
507 1, std::memory_order_relaxed);
508
509 // Retrieve the outgoing arcs for the current label.
510 const auto &to_arcs =
511 nodes[L->node_id].get_arcs<Direction::Forward>();
512 for (const auto &arc : to_arcs) {
513 const int to_node = arc.to;
514 if constexpr (S == Stage::Three ||
515 S == Stage::Eliminate) {
516 if (is_arc_fixed(L->node_id, to_node)) continue;
517 }
518
519 // Extend the label along the current arc.
520 auto extended_labels =
521 Extend<Direction::Forward, S, ArcType::Node,
522 Mutability::Const, Full::Reverse>(L, arc);
523
524 // Process each extended label.
525 for (Label *L_prime : extended_labels) {
526 int bucket_to_process =
527 L_prime->vertex; // mutable copy if needed
528
529 // Concatenate the label with bucket information,
530 // updating best_cost.
531 ConcatenateLabel<S, SYM>(L, bucket_to_process,
532 best_cost);
533 }
534 }
535 }
536 }
537 });
538
539 // Submit the bulk work to the merge scheduler and wait for completion.
540 auto work = stdexec::starts_on(merge_sched, bulk_sender);
541 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));
542
543 // === Post-processing and Sorting ===
544 // Sort merged labels based on cost (ascending order).
545 pdqsort(merged_labels.begin(), merged_labels.end(),
546 [](const Label *a, const Label *b) { return a->cost < b->cost; });
547
548#ifdef SCHRODINGER
549 // If using the Schrödinger approach, process additional paths.
550 if (merged_labels.size() > N_ADD) {
551 std::vector<Path> paths;
552 const int labels_size = merged_labels.size();
553 const int end_idx = std::min(N_ADD + N_ADD, labels_size);
554 paths.reserve(end_idx - N_ADD);
555 for (int i = N_ADD; i < end_idx; ++i) {
556 if (merged_labels[i]->nodes_covered.size() <= 3) continue;
557 paths.emplace_back(merged_labels[i]->nodes_covered,
558 merged_labels[i]->real_cost);
559 }
560 sPool.add_paths(paths);
561 sPool.iterate();
562 }
563#endif
564
565#ifdef RIH
566 // If using RIH stage modifications, submit the top labels to ILS.
567 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
568 std::vector<Label *> top_labels;
569 top_labels.reserve(N_ADD);
570 const int n_candidates =
571 std::min(N_ADD, static_cast<int>(merged_labels.size()));
572 for (int i = 0; i < n_candidates; ++i) {
573 if (merged_labels[i]->nodes_covered.size() <= 3) continue;
574 top_labels.push_back(merged_labels[i]);
575 }
576 ils->submit_task(top_labels, nodes);
577 }
578#endif
579
580 // Store the cost of the best (first) merged label as the inner objective.
581 inner_obj = merged_labels[0]->cost;
582 return merged_labels;
583}

void bucket_fixing()

◆ bucket_fixing()

template<Stage S>
void BucketGraph::bucket_fixing ( )

Fixes the bucket arcs for the specified stage.

This function performs the bucket arc fixing for the given stage. It initializes necessary variables and runs labeling algorithms to compute forward and backward reduced costs. Based on the computed gap, it performs arc elimination in both forward and backward directions and generates the necessary arcs.

Definition at line 1042 of file BucketUtils.h.

1042 {
1043 // Only execute if not already fixed.
1044 if (!fixed) {
1045 fmt::print("\033[34m_STARTING BUCKET FIXING PROCEDURE\033[0m\n");
1046 fixed = true;
1047
1048 // Initialize common structures, arc scores, warm labels, etc.
1049 common_initialization();
1050
1051 // Initialize forward and backward c_bar vectors.
1052 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(
1053 fw_buckets.size(), std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
1054 std::vector<double> backward_cbar(
1055 bw_buckets.size(), std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
1056
1057 // Run labeling algorithms for Stage Four with full mode.
1058 run_labeling_algorithms<Stage::Four, Full::Full>(forward_cbar,
1059 backward_cbar);
1060
1061 // Compute gap and check feasibility (if gap is negative, exit
1062 // early).
1063 gap = std::ceil(incumbent - (relaxation + std::min(0.0, min_red_cost)));
1064 if (gap < 0) {
1065 fmt::print(
1066 "\033[34m_BUCKET FIXING PROCEDURE CAN'T BE EXECUTED DUE TO "
1067 "GAP\033[0m\n");
1068 fmt::print("gap: {}\n", gap);
1069 return;
1070 }
1071
1072 // Save computed c_bar values.
1073 fw_c_bar = forward_cbar;
1074 bw_c_bar = backward_cbar;
1075
1076 // Print info about bucket arc elimination.
1077 print_info("performing bucket arc elimination with theta = {}\n", gap);
1078
1079 // Run forward and backward sections in parallel.
1080 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
1081 work, bi_sched,
1082 SECTION {
1083 // Section 1: Process forward direction.
1084 BucketArcElimination<Direction::Forward>(gap);
1085 ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Forward>();
1086 },
1087 SECTION {
1088 // Section 2: Process backward direction.
1089 BucketArcElimination<Direction::Backward>(gap);
1090 ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Backward>();
1091 });
1092
1093 // Generate arcs after elimination.
1094 generate_arcs();
1095 fmt::print("\033[34m_BUCKET FIXING PROCEDURE FINISHED\033[0m\n");
1096
1097 just_fixed = true;
1098 return;
1099 }
1100 just_fixed = false;
1101}

◆ BucketArcElimination()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::BucketArcElimination ( double theta)

Eliminates bucket arcs based on a given threshold.

This function performs bucket arc elimination in either the forward or backward direction, depending on the template parameter D. It resets all elements in the fixed buckets to 0, prints information about the direction of elimination, and processes arcs to update the local bucket arc map and fixed buckets.

Definition at line 174 of file BucketJump.h.

174 {
175 // Select direction-specific containers.
176 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
177 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
178 auto &Phi_opposite = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_bw, Phi_fw);
179 auto &fixed_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets, bw_fixed_buckets);
180 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
181 auto &fixed_buckets_bitmap =
182 assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets_bitmap, bw_fixed_buckets_bitmap);
183
184 // Reset fixed buckets bitmap.
185 fixed_buckets_bitmap.assign(fixed_buckets_bitmap.size(), 0);
186 const int n_buckets = buckets_size;
187 // Compute the number of 64-bit segments for bitmaps.
188 const size_t n_segments = (buckets_size + 63) / 64;
189
190 // Define ArcMap to store arc information.
191 using ArcMap =
192 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<std::pair<std::pair<int, int>, int>,
193 ankerl::unordered_dense::set<int>,
194 arc_map_hash>;
195 ArcMap local_B_Ba_b;
196 int removed_arcs = 0;
197
198 // Helper: create an arc key from two node IDs and a bucket.
199 auto create_arc_key = [](int from, int to, int b) {
200 return std::make_pair(std::make_pair(from, to), b);
201 };
202
203 // Helper lambda to quickly reset a bitmap vector.
204 auto reset_bitmap = [&](std::vector<uint64_t> &bitmap) {
205 // Using memset since our vector is contiguous.
206 if (!bitmap.empty())
207 std::memset(bitmap.data(), 0, bitmap.size() * sizeof(uint64_t));
208 };
209
210 // Lambda: Process jump arcs for bucket 'b'.
211 auto process_jump_arcs = [&](int b) {
212 const auto &jump_arcs = buckets[b].template get_jump_arcs<D>();
213 if (jump_arcs.empty()) return;
214
215 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments, 0);
216 auto labels = buckets[b].get_labels();
217
218 for (const auto &a : jump_arcs) {
219 auto increment = a.resource_increment;
220 auto arc_key = create_arc_key(a.base_bucket, a.jump_bucket, b);
221 int b_opposite =
222 get_opposite_bucket_number<D>(a.jump_bucket, increment);
223 auto &Bidi_map = local_B_Ba_b[arc_key];
224
225 // Process each label in the current bucket.
226 for (auto &L_item : labels) {
227 Bidi_map.insert(b);
228 // Reset Bvisited for each UpdateBucketsSet call.
229 reset_bitmap(Bvisited);
230 UpdateBucketsSet<D>(theta, L_item, Bidi_map, b_opposite,
231 Bvisited);
232 }
233 }
234 };
235
236 // Lambda: Process bucket arcs for bucket 'b'.
237 auto process_bucket_arcs = [&](int b) {
238 const auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
239 if (bucket_arcs.empty()) return;
240
241 auto labels = buckets[b].get_labels();
242 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments, 0);
243
244 for (const auto &a : bucket_arcs) {
245 // Build the resource increment vector.
246 std::vector<double> increment(options.resources.size(), 0.0);
247 for (size_t r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
248 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward)
249 increment[r] = buckets[b].lb[r] + a.resource_increment[r];
250 else
251 increment[r] = buckets[b].ub[r] - a.resource_increment[r];
252 }
253
254 auto arc_key = create_arc_key(buckets[a.from_bucket].node_id,
255 buckets[a.to_bucket].node_id, b);
256 int b_opposite =
257 get_opposite_bucket_number<D>(a.to_bucket, increment);
258 auto &Bidi_map = local_B_Ba_b[arc_key];
259
260 // Process each label in bucket 'b'.
261 for (auto &L_item : labels) {
262 Bidi_map.insert(b);
263 reset_bitmap(Bvisited);
264 UpdateBucketsSet<D>(theta, L_item, Bidi_map, b_opposite,
265 Bvisited);
266 }
267
268 // Check if the opposite direction contains arcs.
269 if (auto it = local_B_Ba_b.find(arc_key);
270 it != local_B_Ba_b.end()) {
271 auto &Bidi_map_opposite = it->second;
272 bool contains = std::any_of(
273 Phi_opposite[b_opposite].begin(),
274 Phi_opposite[b_opposite].end(), [&](int b_prime) {
275 return Bidi_map_opposite.find(b_prime) !=
276 Bidi_map_opposite.end();
277 });
278 if (!contains && Bidi_map_opposite.find(b_opposite) ==
279 Bidi_map_opposite.end()) {
280 size_t bit_pos =
281 static_cast<size_t>(a.from_bucket) * n_buckets +
282 a.to_bucket;
283 fixed_buckets_bitmap[bit_pos / 64] |=
284 (1ULL << (bit_pos % 64));
285 ++removed_arcs;
286 }
287 }
288 }
289 };
290
291 // Process each bucket.
292 for (int b = 0; b < buckets_size; ++b) {
293 // Process node arcs.
294 const auto &node_arcs =
295 nodes[buckets[b].node_id].template get_arcs<D>();
296 for (const auto &a : node_arcs) {
297 auto arc_key = std::make_pair(std::make_pair(a.from, a.to), b);
298 auto &Bidi_map = local_B_Ba_b[arc_key];
299
300 if (!Phi[b].empty()) {
301 Bidi_map.insert(b);
302 // Merge neighbor bucket arcs.
303 for (const auto &b_prime : Phi[b]) {
304 auto neighbor_key =
305 std::make_pair(std::make_pair(a.from, a.to), b_prime);
306 if (auto it = local_B_Ba_b.find(neighbor_key);
307 it != local_B_Ba_b.end()) {
308 Bidi_map.insert(it->second.begin(), it->second.end());
309 }
310 }
311 }
312 }
313
314 // Process jump arcs and bucket arcs.
315 process_jump_arcs(b);
316 process_bucket_arcs(b);
317 }
318
319 // Print the total number of removed arcs.
320 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward)
321 print_info("[Fw] removed arcs: {}\n", removed_arcs);
322 else
323 print_info("[Bw] removed arcs: {}\n", removed_arcs);
324}

◆ BucketSetContains()

bool BucketGraph::BucketSetContains ( const std::set< int > & bucket_set,
const int & bucket )

Checks if a given bucket is present in the bucket set.

Parameters
bucket_setThe set of buckets to search in.
bucketThe bucket to check for.
Returns
True if the bucket is found in the set, false otherwise.

◆ calculate_neighborhoods()

void BucketGraph::calculate_neighborhoods ( size_t num_closest)

Calculates the neighborhoods for each node for the ng-routes.

Definition at line 285 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

285 {
286 const size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
287
288 // Resize the neighborhoods_bitmap vector so that there's one bitmap per
289 // node.
290 neighborhoods_bitmap.resize(num_nodes);
291
292 // Pre-calculate the number of segments needed (each segment is 64 bits).
293 const size_t num_segments = (num_nodes + 63) / 64;
294
295 // Process each node to compute its neighborhood.
296 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
297 // Create a vector of (distance, node_index) pairs for node i.
298 std::vector<std::pair<double, size_t>> forward_distances;
299 forward_distances.reserve(num_nodes - 1);
300
301 for (size_t j = 0; j < num_nodes; ++j) {
302 if (i == j) continue;
303 double distance = getcij(i, j);
304 forward_distances.push_back({distance, j});
305 }
306
307 // Sort distances to obtain the closest nodes first.
308 pdqsort(forward_distances.begin(), forward_distances.end(),
309 [](const auto &a, const auto &b) { return a.first < b.first; });
310
311 // Initialize the neighborhood bitmap for node i with all segments set
312 // to zero.
313 neighborhoods_bitmap[i].assign(num_segments, 0ULL);
314
315 // Always include the node itself in its neighborhood.
316 size_t self_segment = i >> 6;
317 size_t self_bit = i & 63;
318 neighborhoods_bitmap[i][self_segment] |= (1ULL << self_bit);
319
320 // Add the top 'num_closest' closest nodes as forward neighbors.
321 for (size_t k = 0; k < num_closest && k < forward_distances.size();
322 ++k) {
323 size_t neighbor_index = forward_distances[k].second;
324 size_t segment = neighbor_index >> 6;
325 size_t bit_position = neighbor_index & 63;
326 neighborhoods_bitmap[i][segment] |= (1ULL << bit_position);
327 }
328 }
329}
BucketGraph::getcij
double getcij(int i, int j) const

◆ check_feasibility()

bool BucketGraph::check_feasibility ( const Label * fw_label,
const Label * bw_label )
inline

Checks the feasibility of a given forward and backward label.

This function determines if the transition from a forward label to a backward label is feasible based on resource constraints and node durations.

Definition at line 847 of file BucketGraph.h.

848 {
849 if (!fw_label || !bw_label) return false;
850
851 // Cache resources and node data
852 const auto &fw_resources = fw_label->resources;
853 const auto &bw_resources = bw_label->resources;
854 const struct VRPNode &VRPNode = nodes[fw_label->node_id];
855
856 // Time feasibility check
857 for (auto r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
858 if (options.resources[r] != "time") {
859 continue;
860 }
861 const auto time_fw = fw_resources[r];
862 const auto time_bw = bw_resources[r];
863 const auto travel_time =
864 getcij(fw_label->node_id, bw_label->node_id);
865 if (time_fw + travel_time + VRPNode.duration > time_bw) {
866 return false;
867 }
868 }
869
870 // Resource feasibility check (if applicable)
871 if (options.resources.size() > 1) {
872 for (size_t i = 1; i < options.resources.size(); ++i) {
873 const auto resource_fw = fw_resources[i];
874 const auto resource_bw = bw_resources[i];
875 const auto demand = VRPNode.demand;
876
877 if (resource_fw + demand > resource_bw) {
878 return false;
879 }
880 }
881 }
882
883 return true;
884 }

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::common_initialization ( )

Initializes the BucketGraph by clearing previous data and setting up forward and backward buckets.

1394 {
1395 // --- Pre-allocate and clear temporary vectors ---
1396 merged_labels.clear();
1397 merged_labels.reserve(50);
1398
1399 // Retrieve active cuts and build a vector of ActiveCutInfo.
1400 auto &cutter = cut_storage;
1401 const auto active_cuts = cutter->getActiveCuts();
1402 std::vector<ActiveCutInfo> active_cuts_info;
1403 active_cuts_info.reserve(active_cuts.size());
1404 for (const auto &cut : active_cuts) {
1405 active_cuts_info.push_back(cut);
1406 }
1407
1408 // --- Sort arcs by scores for each node ---
1409 auto &arc_scores = assign_buckets<D>(fw_arc_scores, bw_arc_scores);
1410 for (auto &node : nodes) {
1411 if (!arc_scores[node.id].empty()) {
1412 node.sort_arcs_by_scores<D>(arc_scores[node.id], nodes,
1413 active_cuts_info);
1414 }
1415 arc_scores[node.id].clear();
1416 }
1417
1418 // --- Warm start: collect best labels from buckets ---
1419 auto &warm_labels = assign_buckets<D>(fw_warm_labels, bw_warm_labels);
1420 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
1421 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
1422 auto &c_bar = assign_buckets<D>(fw_c_bar, bw_c_bar);
1423 auto &bucket_splits = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_splits, bw_bucket_splits);
1424
1425 if (!merged_labels.empty() && options.warm_start && !just_fixed) {
1426 warm_labels.clear();
1427 warm_labels.reserve(buckets_size);
1428 for (int bucket = 0; bucket < buckets_size; ++bucket) {
1429 if (auto *label = buckets[bucket].get_best_label()) {
1430 warm_labels.push_back(label);
1431 }
1432 }
1433 }
1434
1435 // --- Initialize c_bar vector ---
1436 c_bar.resize(buckets_size, std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
1437
1438 // --- Initialize dominance check counters for forward direction ---
1439 if constexpr (Direction::Forward == D) {
1440 dominance_checks_per_bucket.assign(buckets_size + 1, 0);
1441 non_dominated_labels_per_bucket = 0;
1442 }
1443
1444 // --- Clear all buckets ---
1445 for (size_t b = 0; b < buckets_size; b++) {
1446 buckets[b].clear();
1447 }
1448
1449 // --- Warm start processing: sort warm_labels and compute reduced costs
1450 // ---
1451 if (options.warm_start && !just_fixed) {
1452 pdqsort(
1453 warm_labels.begin(), warm_labels.end(),
1454 [](const Label *a, const Label *b) { return a->cost < b->cost; });
1455
1456 std::vector<Label *> processed_labels;
1457 const size_t process_size = std::min(
1458 warm_labels.size(),
1459 static_cast<size_t>(options.n_warm_start * warm_labels.size()));
1460 for (size_t i = 0; i < process_size; ++i) {
1461 auto label = warm_labels[i];
1462 // Uncomment if you want to skip non-fresh labels.
1463 if (!label->fresh) continue;
1464 auto new_label = compute_red_cost(label, D == Direction::Forward);
1465 if (new_label != nullptr) {
1466 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
1467 fw_buckets[new_label->vertex].add_sorted_label(new_label);
1468 } else {
1469 bw_buckets[new_label->vertex].add_sorted_label(new_label);
1470 }
1471 processed_labels.push_back(new_label);
1472 }
1473 }
1474 warm_labels = std::move(processed_labels);
1475 }
1476
1477 // --- Prepare interval arrays and compute per-resource splits for depot ---
1478 const size_t num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
1479 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals);
1480 std::vector<double> interval_starts(num_intervals);
1481 std::vector<double> interval_ends(num_intervals);
1482 // Compute per-resource split counts for the depot
1483 const auto &VRPNode = nodes[0]; // assuming node 0 is the depot
1484 std::vector<int> depot_splits(num_intervals);
1485 for (size_t r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
1486 // Use the same ratio as earlier:
1487 double full_range = R_max[r] - R_min[r];
1488 double node_range = VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r];
1489 depot_splits[r] =
1490 std::max(1, static_cast<int>(std::round((node_range / full_range) *
1491 intervals[r].interval)));
1492 // Compute the base interval for resource r.
1493 base_intervals[r] = (VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r]) / depot_splits[r];
1494 interval_starts[r] = VRPNode.lb[r];
1495 interval_ends[r] = VRPNode.ub[r];
1496 }
1497
1498 // --- Interval-based bucket generation ---
1499 std::vector<int> current_pos(num_intervals, 0);
1500 int offset = 0;
1501 // Lambda for generating interval combinations and creating depots.
1502 auto generate_combinations = [&](bool is_forward, int &offset) {
1503 auto &label_pool = is_forward ? label_pool_fw : label_pool_bw;
1504 auto &buckets = is_forward ? fw_buckets : bw_buckets;
1505 const auto depot_id = is_forward ? options.depot : options.end_depot;
1506 const int calculated_index_base =
1507 is_forward ? num_buckets_index_fw[options.depot]
1508 : num_buckets_index_bw[options.end_depot];
1509
1510 std::vector<double> interval_bounds(num_intervals);
1511 // Recursive lambda to generate all interval combinations.
1512 std::function<void(int)> process_intervals = [&](int depth) {
1513 if (depth == num_intervals) {
1514 // Acquire a new depot label.
1515 auto depot = label_pool->acquire();
1516 int calculated_index = calculated_index_base + offset;
1517 for (int r = 0; r < static_cast<int>(num_intervals); ++r) {
1518 // For forward direction, start at the lower bound and add
1519 // multiples of the base interval; adjust similarly for
1520 // backward.
1521 interval_bounds[r] =
1522 is_forward ? interval_starts[r] +
1523 current_pos[r] * base_intervals[r]
1524 : interval_ends[r] -
1525 current_pos[r] * base_intervals[r];
1526 }
1527 depot->initialize(calculated_index, 0.0, interval_bounds,
1528 depot_id);
1529 depot->is_extended = false;
1530 depot->nodes_covered.push_back(depot_id);
1531 set_node_visited(depot->visited_bitmap, depot_id);
1532 SRC_MODE_BLOCK(
1533 depot->SRCmap.assign(cut_storage->SRCDuals.size(), 0);)
1534 buckets[calculated_index].add_label(depot);
1535 buckets[calculated_index].node_id = depot_id;
1536
1537 offset++;
1538 return;
1539 }
1540
1541 // For the current resource dimension, loop using its specific split
1542 // count.
1543 for (int k = 0; k < depot_splits[depth]; ++k) {
1544 current_pos[depth] = k;
1545 process_intervals(depth + 1);
1546 }
1547 };
1548 process_intervals(0);
1549 };
1550
1551 // Generate combinations for the current direction.
1552 generate_combinations(D == Direction::Forward, offset);
1553}
SRC_MODE_BLOCK
#define SRC_MODE_BLOCK(code_block)

template<Stage S>
Label * BucketGraph::compute_label ( const Label * L,
const Label * L_prime,
double red_cost = 0.0 )

Computes a new label based on the given labels L and L_prime.

Definition at line 1066 of file BucketSolve.h.

1067 {
1068 // Compute cost values
1069 double cij_cost = getcij(L->node_id, L_prime->node_id);
1070 double real_cost = L->real_cost + L_prime->real_cost + cij_cost;
1071
1072 // Acquire a new label from the pool and initialize its cost fields.
1073 auto new_label = new Label();
1074 new_label->cost = red_cost;
1075 new_label->real_cost = real_cost;
1076
1077 // Efficiently combine nodes_covered: preallocate sufficient space to avoid
1078 // reallocation.
1079 new_label->nodes_covered.clear();
1080 new_label->nodes_covered.reserve(L->nodes_covered.size() +
1081 L_prime->nodes_covered.size());
1082
1083 // First add the nodes from L.
1084 new_label->nodes_covered.insert(new_label->nodes_covered.end(),
1085 L->nodes_covered.begin(),
1086 L->nodes_covered.end());
1087
1088 // Then add the nodes from L_prime in reverse order using reverse iterators.
1089 new_label->nodes_covered.insert(new_label->nodes_covered.end(),
1090 L_prime->nodes_covered.rbegin(),
1091 L_prime->nodes_covered.rend());
1092
1093 return new_label;
1094}

◆ compute_mono_label()

Label * BucketGraph::compute_mono_label ( const Label * L)

Computes the mono label for the BucketGraph.

This function computes the mono label for the BucketGraph by acquiring a new label from the label pool and setting the cost and real cost values from the given label L. It then calculates the number of nodes covered by the label and its ancestors, reserves space for the nodes covered, and inserts the nodes from the

◆ compute_red_cost()

Label * BucketGraph::compute_red_cost ( Label * L,
bool fw )
inline

Definition at line 1174 of file BucketGraph.h.

1174 {
1175 // Calculate main and reduced costs.
1176 double real_cost = 0.0;
1177 double red_cost = 0.0;
1178
1179 // Check main resource feasibility.
1180 const double main_resource = L->resources[options.main_resources[0]];
1181 const double q_star_value = q_star[options.main_resources[0]];
1182 if (fw) {
1183 if (main_resource > q_star_value) return nullptr;
1184 } else {
1185 if (main_resource <= q_star_value) return nullptr;
1186 }
1187
1188 // Ensure sufficient nodes in the route.
1189 if (L->nodes_covered.size() <= 3) return nullptr;
1190
1191 // Initialize an empty SRCmap vector with size equal to the current
1192 // number of cuts.
1193 std::vector<uint16_t> updated_SRCmap(cut_storage->size(), 0);
1194
1195 // Prepare new label.
1196 auto new_label = new Label();
1197 new_label->nodes_covered.clear();
1198 new_label->nodes_covered = L->nodes_covered; // Copy the route
1199
1200 // Variables to keep track of costs.
1201 int last_node = -1;
1202
1203 // Traverse the nodes in the route (from current label to root).
1204 for (auto node_id : L->nodes_covered) {
1205 // --- NG-route Feasibility Check ---
1206 // Clear visited bitmap in the neighborhood of node_id.
1207 for (size_t seg = 0, limit = new_label->visited_bitmap.size();
1208 seg < limit; ++seg) {
1209 uint64_t current_visited = new_label->visited_bitmap[seg];
1210 if (!current_visited) continue;
1211 uint64_t neighborhood_mask = neighborhoods_bitmap[node_id][seg];
1212 new_label->visited_bitmap[seg] =
1213 current_visited & neighborhood_mask;
1214 }
1215 set_node_visited(new_label->visited_bitmap, node_id);
1216
1217 // --- Cost Accumulation ---
1218 if (last_node != -1) {
1219 double cij_cost = getcij(last_node, node_id);
1220 real_cost += cij_cost;
1221 red_cost += cij_cost;
1222 }
1223 red_cost -= nodes[node_id].cost;
1224
1225 // --- SRC Logic ---
1226 size_t segment = node_id >> 6;
1227 const uint64_t bit_mask = bit_mask_lookup[node_id & 63];
1228 auto &cutter = cut_storage;
1229 auto &SRCDuals = cutter->SRCDuals;
1230
1231#if defined(SRC)
1232 for (std::size_t idx = 0; idx < cutter->size(); ++idx) {
1233 const auto &cut = cutter->getCut(idx);
1234 // Retrieve base set and order information.
1235 const auto &baseSet = cut.baseSet;
1236 const auto &baseSetOrder = cut.baseSetOrder;
1237 const auto &neighbors = cut.neighbors;
1238 const auto &multipliers = cut.p;
1239
1240 // If the current node is not in the neighbor set, reset SRCmap
1241 // value.
1242 if (!(neighbors[segment] & bit_mask)) {
1243 updated_SRCmap[idx] = 0;
1244 continue;
1245 }
1246 // If the current node is in the base set, update SRC value.
1247 if (baseSet[segment] & bit_mask) {
1248 auto &src_val = updated_SRCmap[idx];
1249 src_val += multipliers.num[baseSetOrder[node_id]];
1250 if (src_val >= multipliers.den) {
1251 red_cost -= SRCDuals[idx];
1252 src_val -= multipliers.den;
1253 }
1254 }
1255 }
1256#endif
1257
1258 // --- Arc and Branching Dual Adjustments ---
1259#if defined(RCC) || defined(EXACT_RCC)
1260 if (last_node != -1) {
1261 red_cost -= arc_duals.getDual(last_node, node_id);
1262 }
1263#endif
1264 if (branching_duals->size() > 0 && last_node != -1) {
1265 red_cost -= branching_duals->getDual(last_node, node_id);
1266 }
1267
1268 last_node = node_id; // Update last_node.
1269 }
1270
1271 // Set final label properties.
1272 new_label->cost = red_cost;
1273 new_label->real_cost = real_cost;
1274 new_label->parent = nullptr;
1275 new_label->node_id = L->node_id;
1276 new_label->is_extended = false;
1277 new_label->resources = L->resources;
1278 new_label->SRCmap = updated_SRCmap;
1279 new_label->fresh = false;
1280
1281 // Determine bucket number for the new label.
1282 std::vector<double> new_resources(options.resources.size());
1283 for (size_t i = 0; i < options.resources.size(); ++i) {
1284 new_resources[i] = new_label->resources[i];
1285 }
1286 int bucket = fw ? get_bucket_number<Direction::Forward>(
1287 new_label->node_id, new_resources)
1288 : get_bucket_number<Direction::Backward>(
1289 new_label->node_id, new_resources);
1290 new_label->vertex = bucket;
1291
1292 return new_label;
1293 }

◆ computePhi()

std::vector< int > BucketGraph::computePhi ( int & bucket_id,
bool fw )

Computes the phi values for a given bucket ID and direction.

Definition at line 196 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

196 {
197 std::vector<int> phi;
198
199 // Get references based on the direction.
200 auto &buckets = fw ? fw_buckets : bw_buckets;
201 auto &node_interval_trees =
202 fw ? fw_node_interval_trees : bw_node_interval_trees;
203
204 // Multi-dimensional resource case.
205 if (options.resources.size() > 1) {
206 if (bucket_id < 0 || bucket_id >= static_cast<int>(buckets.size()))
207 return phi;
208
209 const int n_dims = static_cast<int>(intervals.size());
210 std::vector<double> base_intervals(n_dims, 0.0);
211
212 // Get the node associated with the current bucket.
213 int node_id = buckets[bucket_id].node_id;
214 const VRPNode &node = nodes[node_id];
215
216 // Recompute splits and base intervals per dimension using the same
217 // logic as in define_buckets.
218 for (int r = 0; r < n_dims; ++r) {
219 double full_range = R_max[r] - R_min[r];
220 double node_range = node.ub[r] - node.lb[r];
221 int splits = 1;
222 if (std::fabs(full_range) >
223 std::numeric_limits<double>::epsilon()) {
224 double splits_d =
225 (node_range * intervals[r].interval) / full_range;
226 splits = std::max(1, static_cast<int>(std::round(splits_d)));
227 }
228 base_intervals[r] = node_range / static_cast<double>(splits);
229 }
230
231 // Start with the current bucket's baseline.
232 const auto &current_bucket = buckets[bucket_id];
233 std::vector<double> target = fw ? current_bucket.lb : current_bucket.ub;
234
235 // Adjust the target vector by one base interval for each dimension.
236 // For forward, shift left (i.e. subtract) but do not go below the
237 // node's lb. For backward, shift right (i.e. add) but do not exceed the
238 // node's ub.
239 for (int r = 0; r < n_dims; ++r) {
240 if (fw)
241 target[r] = std::max(node.lb[r], target[r] - base_intervals[r]);
242 else
243 target[r] = std::min(node.ub[r], target[r] + base_intervals[r]);
244 }
245
246 // Look up the candidate bucket in the node's interval tree.
247 TreeNode *found_node = node_interval_trees[node_id].find(target);
248 if (found_node != nullptr &&
249 buckets[found_node->bucket_index].node_id == node_id) {
250#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
251 bool not_fixed = fw ? is_bucket_not_fixed_forward(
252 bucket_id, found_node->bucket_index)
253 : is_bucket_not_fixed_backward(
254 bucket_id, found_node->bucket_index);
255 if (not_fixed)
256#endif
257 {
258 phi.push_back(found_node->bucket_index);
259 }
260 }
261 }
262 // Single-resource case (simpler).
263 else {
264 int neighbor = bucket_id - 1;
265 if (neighbor >= 0 && neighbor < static_cast<int>(buckets.size()) &&
266 buckets[neighbor].node_id == buckets[bucket_id].node_id) {
267#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
268 bool not_fixed =
269 fw ? is_bucket_not_fixed_forward(neighbor, bucket_id)
270 : is_bucket_not_fixed_backward(neighbor, bucket_id);
271 if (not_fixed)
272#endif
273 {
274 phi.push_back(neighbor);
275 }
276 }
277 }
278 return phi;
279}

template<Stage S, Symmetry SYM>
void BucketGraph::ConcatenateLabel ( const Label * L,
int & b,
std::atomic< double > & best_cost )

Concatenates the label L with the bucket b and updates the best label pbest.

Definition at line 653 of file BucketUtils.h.

654 {
655 // Determine the number of segments needed for the bucket visited bitmap.
656 const size_t n_segments = (fw_buckets_size + 63) / 64;
657 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments, 0);
658
659 // Use thread_local storage for the bucket stack to avoid reallocations.
660 static thread_local std::vector<int> bucket_stack;
661 bucket_stack.reserve(R_SIZE); // Reserve once at initialization
662 bucket_stack.clear(); // Clear for reuse
663 bucket_stack.push_back(b);
664
665 // Choose symmetric buckets and cost-bar arrays based on symmetry.
666 auto &other_buckets = assign_symmetry<SYM>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
667 auto &other_c_bar = assign_symmetry<SYM>(fw_c_bar, bw_c_bar);
668
669 // Cache frequently accessed values.
670 const int L_node_id = L->node_id;
671 const auto &L_resources = L->resources;
672 const auto &L_last_node = nodes[L_node_id];
673 const double L_cost = L->cost;
674 const size_t bitmap_size = L->visited_bitmap.size();
675
676 // Pre-compute if branching is active.
677 const bool has_branching = !branching_duals->empty();
678
679#if defined(SRC)
680 // SRC mode setup: Only relevant in Stage::Four.
681 decltype(cut_storage) cutter = nullptr;
682 if constexpr (S > Stage::Three) {
683 cutter = cut_storage;
684 }
685 const auto active_cuts = cutter->getActiveCuts();
686#endif
687
688 // Lambda to check if the visited bitmaps of L and L_bw overlap.
689 auto overlapsVisited = [bitmap_size](const Label *L,
690 const Label *L_bw) -> bool {
691 for (size_t i = 0; i < bitmap_size; ++i) {
692 if (L->visited_bitmap[i] & L_bw->visited_bitmap[i]) {
693 return true;
694 }
695 }
696 return false;
697 };
698
699 // Lambda to apply SRC adjustments to total_cost (only for Stage::Four).
700#if defined(SRC)
701 auto applySRCAdjustments = [&](double total_cost, const Label *L,
702 const Label *L_bw) -> double {
703 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
704 for (const auto &active_cut : active_cuts) {
705 const auto &cut = *active_cut.cut_ptr;
706 const size_t idx = active_cut.index;
707 const double dual = active_cut.dual_value;
708 if (L->SRCmap[idx] + L_bw->SRCmap[idx] >= cut.p.den) {
709 total_cost -= dual;
710 }
711 }
712 }
713 return total_cost;
714 };
715#endif
716
717 // Process buckets until the stack is empty.
718 while (!bucket_stack.empty()) {
719 const int current_bucket = bucket_stack.back();
720 bucket_stack.pop_back();
721
722 // Mark current_bucket as visited in the bitmap.
723 const size_t segment = current_bucket >> 6;
724 const uint64_t bit_mask = bit_mask_lookup[current_bucket & 63];
725 Bvisited[segment] |= bit_mask;
726
727 // Get the node id associated with this bucket.
728 const int bucketLprimenode = other_buckets[current_bucket].node_id;
729 double travel_cost = getcij(L_node_id, bucketLprimenode);
730
731#if defined(RCC) || defined(EXACT_RCC)
732 // Adjust travel cost with arc duals in Stage Four.
733 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
734 travel_cost -= arc_duals.getDual(L_node_id, bucketLprimenode);
735 }
736#endif
737
738 if (has_branching) {
739 travel_cost -=
740 branching_duals->getDual(L_node_id, bucketLprimenode);
741 }
742
743 const double path_cost = L_cost + travel_cost;
744 const double bound = other_c_bar[current_bucket];
745
746 // Early bound check.
747 if ((S != Stage::Enumerate && path_cost + bound >= best_cost.load()) ||
748 (S == Stage::Enumerate && path_cost + bound >= gap)) {
749 continue;
750 }
751
752 // Retrieve labels in the current bucket.
753 const auto &bucket = other_buckets[current_bucket];
754 const auto labels = bucket.get_labels();
755 if (labels.empty()) continue;
756
757 // Process each label in the current bucket.
758 for (const Label *L_bw : labels) {
759 // Skip null labels, dominated labels, same node, or infeasible
760 // merges.
761 if (L_bw == nullptr || L_bw->is_dominated ||
762 L_bw->node_id == L_node_id || !check_feasibility(L, L_bw)) {
763 continue;
764 }
765
766 // If in Stage Three or later, check for visited node overlap.
767 if constexpr (S >= Stage::Three) {
768 if (overlapsVisited(L, L_bw)) continue;
769 }
770
771 double total_cost = path_cost + L_bw->cost;
772
773#if defined(SRC)
774 // Apply SRC adjustments if in Stage Four.
775 total_cost = applySRCAdjustments(total_cost, L, L_bw);
776#endif
777 double current_best = best_cost.load();
778
779 // Filter based on total cost against best known cost or gap.
780 if ((S != Stage::Enumerate && total_cost >= current_best) ||
781 (S == Stage::Enumerate && total_cost >= gap)) {
782 continue;
783 }
784
785 // Create and merge a new label. Protect the merge operation.
786 // Create and merge a new label. Protect the merge operation.
787 {
788 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(merge_mutex);
789
790 // Update best_cost reliably using a compare_exchange loop.
791 double expected = best_cost.load();
792 while (total_cost < expected &&
793 !best_cost.compare_exchange_weak(expected, total_cost)) {
794 // 'expected' is updated with the current value of
795 // best_cost.
796 }
797
798 // If our total_cost is still an improvement, then add the new
799 // label.
800 if (total_cost <= best_cost.load()) {
801 auto pbest = compute_label<S>(L, L_bw, total_cost);
802 merged_labels.push_back(pbest);
803 }
804 }
805 }
806
807 // Add neighbor buckets to the stack if they have not been visited.
808 for (int b_prime : Phi_bw[current_bucket]) {
809 const size_t seg_prime = b_prime >> 6;
810 const uint64_t mask_prime = bit_mask_lookup[b_prime & 63];
811 if (!(Bvisited[seg_prime] & mask_prime)) {
812 bucket_stack.push_back(b_prime);
813 }
814 }
815 }
816}

◆ define_buckets() [1/2]

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::define_buckets ( )

Defines the buckets for the BucketGraph.

This function determines the number of buckets based on the time intervals and assigns buckets to the graph. It computes resource bounds for each vertex and defines the bounds of each bucket.

Definition at line 166 of file BucketUtils.h.

166 {
167 // Save current buckets and fixed status.
168 std::vector<Bucket> old_buckets;
169 std::vector<bool> was_fixed;
170 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
171 size_t old_size = buckets.size();
172
173 // Map to store fixed range (min_lb and max_ub) for each node pair.
174 std::unordered_map<
175 int, std::unordered_map<
176 int, std::pair<std::vector<double>, std::vector<double>>>>
177 node_pair_fixed_ranges;
178
179 if (old_size > 0) {
180 old_buckets = buckets;
181 was_fixed.resize(old_size * old_size, false);
182 auto &old_bitmap =
183 assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets_bitmap, bw_fixed_buckets_bitmap);
184
185 // Mark which bucket pairs were fixed.
186 for (size_t i = 0; i < old_size; ++i) {
187 for (size_t j = 0; j < old_size; ++j) {
188 size_t pos = i * old_size + j;
189 if (pos / 64 < old_bitmap.size()) {
190 was_fixed[pos] =
191 ((old_bitmap[pos / 64] & (1ULL << (pos % 64))) != 0);
192 }
193 }
194 }
195
196 const int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
197 // For each fixed bucket pair, update the range for the corresponding
198 // node pair.
199 for (size_t i = 0; i < old_size; ++i) {
200 for (size_t j = 0; j < old_size; ++j) {
201 if (!was_fixed[i * old_size + j]) continue;
202 const auto &bucket_from = old_buckets[i];
203 const auto &bucket_to = old_buckets[j];
204 int from_node = bucket_from.node_id;
205 int to_node = bucket_to.node_id;
206
207 // Initialize fixed range for this node pair if not already set.
208 if (node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].find(to_node) ==
209 node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].end()) {
210 node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node][to_node] = {
211 std::vector<double>(num_intervals,
212 std::numeric_limits<double>::max()),
213 std::vector<double>(
214 num_intervals,
215 std::numeric_limits<double>::lowest())};
216 }
217 auto &[min_lbs, max_ubs] =
218 node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node][to_node];
219 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
220 min_lbs[r] = std::min(min_lbs[r], bucket_from.lb[r]);
221 max_ubs[r] = std::max(max_ubs[r], bucket_from.ub[r]);
222 }
223 }
224 }
225 } // end if (old_size > 0)
226
227 // Clear existing buckets and the fixed bitmap.
228 buckets.clear();
229 auto &fixed_buckets_bitmap =
230 assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets_bitmap, bw_fixed_buckets_bitmap);
231 fixed_buckets_bitmap.clear();
232
233 const int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
234 std::vector<double> total_ranges(num_intervals);
235 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals);
236
237 // Get references to direction-specific containers.
238 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
239 auto &num_buckets_index =
240 assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
241 auto &node_interval_trees =
242 assign_buckets<D>(fw_node_interval_trees, bw_node_interval_trees);
243 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
244 auto &bucket_splits = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_splits, bw_bucket_splits);
245
246 // Pre-allocate node-specific containers.
247 const size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
248 num_buckets.resize(num_nodes);
249 num_buckets_index.resize(num_nodes);
250 node_interval_trees.assign(num_nodes, SplayTree());
251
252 // Lambda to calculate an interval for a given dimension.
253 // We compute raw boundaries then apply rounding only after ensuring
254 // that the first/last intervals use the original bounds.
255 auto calculate_interval =
256 [&](double lb, double ub, double base_interval, int pos,
257 int max_interval, bool is_forward) -> std::pair<double, double> {
258 double start, end;
259 if (is_forward) {
260 // For the first interval, start exactly at lb.
261 start = (pos == 0) ? lb : lb + pos * base_interval;
262 // For the last interval, end exactly at ub.
263 end =
264 (pos == max_interval - 1) ? ub : lb + (pos + 1) * base_interval;
265 } else {
266 // For Backward: first interval gets ub exactly, last gets lb
267 // exactly.
268 start =
269 (pos == max_interval - 1) ? lb : ub - (pos + 1) * base_interval;
270 end = (pos == 0) ? ub : ub - pos * base_interval;
271 }
272 return {start, end};
273 };
274
275 int cum_sum = 0;
276 int bucket_index = 0;
277 std::vector<double> interval_start(num_intervals);
278 std::vector<double> interval_end(num_intervals);
279 // "pos" will hold the current combination index for each dimension.
280 std::vector<int> pos(num_intervals, 0);
281
282 // Process each node to define buckets.
283 for (const auto &VRPNode : nodes) {
284 // For each resource, compute a node-specific split count.
285 std::vector<int> node_split_counts(num_intervals);
286 std::vector<double> node_base_interval(num_intervals);
287 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
288 double full_range = R_max[r] - R_min[r];
289 double node_range = VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r];
290 int splits = 1;
291 // Guard against a near-zero full_range to avoid division issues.
292 if (std::fabs(full_range) >
293 std::numeric_limits<double>::epsilon()) {
294 // Multiply first for improved stability.
295 double splits_d =
296 (node_range * intervals[r].interval) / full_range;
297 splits = std::max(1, static_cast<int>(std::round(splits_d)));
298 }
299 node_split_counts[r] = splits;
300 // Compute the base interval (width) for this resource dimension.
301 node_base_interval[r] =
302 (VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r]) / static_cast<double>(splits);
303 }
304 // Optionally, store the per-resource splits for this node.
305 bucket_splits[VRPNode.id] =
306 node_split_counts[0]; // or store the full vector if needed
307
308 // SplayTree node_tree;
309 int n_buckets = 0;
310
311 if (num_intervals == 1) {
312 // Single dimension: iterate over the split count for resource 0.
313 for (int j = 0; j < node_split_counts[0]; ++j) {
314 auto [start, end] = calculate_interval(
315 VRPNode.lb[0], VRPNode.ub[0], node_base_interval[0], j,
316 node_split_counts[0], D == Direction::Forward);
317 // Ensure we do not exceed the actual resource range.
318 if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
319 start = std::max(start, VRPNode.lb[0]);
320 } else {
321 end = std::min(end, VRPNode.ub[0]);
322 }
323 buckets.push_back(Bucket(VRPNode.id, std::vector<double>{start},
324 std::vector<double>{end}));
325 // node_tree.insert(std::vector<double>{start},
326 // std::vector<double>{end}, bucket_index++);
327 n_buckets++;
328 cum_sum++;
329 }
330 } else {
331 // Multi-dimensional: generate combinations over each resource.
332 fmt::print("Multiple intervals\n");
333 // Reset the combination index vector.
334 std::fill(pos.begin(), pos.end(), 0);
335 while (true) {
336 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
337 auto [start, end] = calculate_interval(
338 VRPNode.lb[r], VRPNode.ub[r], node_base_interval[r],
339 pos[r], node_split_counts[r], D == Direction::Forward);
340 interval_start[r] = start;
341 interval_end[r] = end;
342 // Clamp to global resource bounds.
343 if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
344 interval_start[r] =
345 std::max(interval_start[r], R_min[r]);
346 } else {
347 interval_end[r] = std::min(interval_end[r], R_max[r]);
348 }
349 }
350 buckets.push_back(
351 Bucket(VRPNode.id, interval_start, interval_end));
352 // node_tree.insert(interval_start, interval_end,
353 // bucket_index++);
354 n_buckets++;
355 cum_sum++;
356
357 // Generate next combination:
358 int i = 0;
359 while (i < num_intervals) {
360 ++pos[i];
361 if (pos[i] < node_split_counts[i]) {
362 break;
363 } else {
364 pos[i] = 0;
365 ++i;
366 }
367 }
368 if (i == num_intervals) break;
369 }
370 }
371
372 // Update node-specific information.
373 num_buckets[VRPNode.id] = n_buckets;
374 num_buckets_index[VRPNode.id] = cum_sum - n_buckets;
375 // node_interval_trees[VRPNode.id] = node_tree;
376 }
377
378 // Process fixed bucket ranges, if available.
379 if (!node_pair_fixed_ranges.empty()) {
380 size_t new_size = buckets.size();
381 size_t required_bitmap_words =
382 std::max(size_t(1), ((new_size * new_size) + 63) / 64);
383 fixed_buckets_bitmap.clear();
384 fixed_buckets_bitmap.resize(required_bitmap_words, 0);
385 for (size_t from = 0; from < new_size; ++from) {
386 const auto &bucket_from = buckets[from];
387 int from_node = bucket_from.node_id;
388 if (node_pair_fixed_ranges.find(from_node) ==
389 node_pair_fixed_ranges.end())
390 continue;
391 for (size_t to = 0; to < new_size; ++to) {
392 const auto &bucket_to = buckets[to];
393 int to_node = bucket_to.node_id;
394 if (node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].find(to_node) ==
395 node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node].end())
396 continue;
397 const auto &range = node_pair_fixed_ranges[from_node][to_node];
398 bool in_range = true;
399 for (int r = 0; r < options.main_resources.size(); ++r) {
400 // Using <= and >= to ensure we cover the full fixed range.
401 if (bucket_from.lb[r] < range.first[r] ||
402 bucket_from.ub[r] > range.second[r]) {
403 in_range = false;
404 break;
405 }
406 }
407 if (in_range) {
408 size_t bit_pos = from * new_size + to;
409 size_t word_idx = bit_pos / 64;
410 if (word_idx < fixed_buckets_bitmap.size()) {
411 fixed_buckets_bitmap[word_idx] |=
412 (1ULL << (bit_pos % 64));
413 }
414 }
415 }
416 }
417 }
418
419 buckets_size = buckets.size();
420}

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::define_buckets ( const int fixed_num_buckets)
inline

Definition at line 1399 of file BucketGraph.h.

1399 {
1400 // Get references to forward/backward buckets.
1401 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
1402 buckets.clear();
1403
1404 // Also clear the fixed bitmap (if used elsewhere).
1405 auto &fixed_buckets_bitmap =
1406 assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets_bitmap, bw_fixed_buckets_bitmap);
1407 fixed_buckets_bitmap.clear();
1408
1409 // Get other direction-specific containers.
1410 const int num_intervals =
1411 options.main_resources.size(); // number of resource dimensions
1412 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
1413 auto &num_buckets_index =
1414 assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
1415 auto &node_interval_trees =
1416 assign_buckets<D>(fw_node_interval_trees, bw_node_interval_trees);
1417 auto &buckets_size =
1418 assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
1419 auto &bucket_splits =
1420 assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_splits, bw_bucket_splits);
1421
1422 // Pre-allocate per-node containers.
1423 const size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
1424 num_buckets.resize(num_nodes);
1425 num_buckets_index.resize(num_nodes);
1426 node_interval_trees.assign(num_nodes, SplayTree());
1427
1428 // Lambda to calculate an interval for a given resource dimension.
1429 // Rounding is applied after computing boundaries.
1430 auto calculate_interval =
1431 [&](double lb, double ub, double base_interval, int pos,
1432 int max_interval,
1433 bool is_forward) -> std::pair<double, double> {
1434 double start, end;
1435 if (is_forward) {
1436 start = (pos == 0) ? lb : lb + pos * base_interval;
1437 end = (pos == max_interval - 1)
1438 ? ub
1439 : lb + (pos + 1) * base_interval;
1440 } else {
1441 start = (pos == max_interval - 1)
1442 ? lb
1443 : ub - (pos + 1) * base_interval;
1444 end = (pos == 0) ? ub : ub - pos * base_interval;
1445 }
1446 return {roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(start),
1447 roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(end)};
1448 };
1449
1450 int bucket_index = 0;
1451 int cum_sum = 0;
1452 std::vector<double> interval_start(num_intervals);
1453 std::vector<double> interval_end(num_intervals);
1454 // "pos" holds the current combination indices when multi-dimensional.
1455 std::vector<int> pos(num_intervals, 0);
1456
1457 // Process each node (job) to define its buckets.
1458 for (const auto &VRPNode : nodes) {
1459 // Compute the number of splits for each resource dimension.
1460 // For a single dimension, we use the fixed number directly.
1461 // For multiple dimensions we choose an equal split in each
1462 // dimension.
1463 std::vector<int> node_split_counts(num_intervals, 1);
1464 if (num_intervals == 1) {
1465 node_split_counts[0] = fixed_num_buckets;
1466 } else {
1467 // Compute an equal split count per dimension (rounded).
1468 int equal_split =
1469 std::max(1, static_cast<int>(std::round(std::pow(
1470 fixed_num_buckets, 1.0 / num_intervals))));
1471 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
1472 node_split_counts[r] = equal_split;
1473 }
1474 // Adjust the first dimension if the product is less than
1475 // fixed_num_buckets.
1476 int product = 1;
1477 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
1478 product *= node_split_counts[r];
1479 }
1480 if (product < fixed_num_buckets) {
1481 int factor = fixed_num_buckets / product;
1482 node_split_counts[0] *= factor;
1483 }
1484 }
1485
1486 // Compute the width (base interval) for each resource dimension.
1487 std::vector<double> node_base_interval(num_intervals);
1488 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
1489 node_base_interval[r] =
1490 (VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r]) /
1491 static_cast<double>(node_split_counts[r]);
1492 }
1493
1494 // Optionally store the per-node split information.
1495 // (Here we store just the first dimension's split count; you could
1496 // store the full vector.)
1497 bucket_splits[VRPNode.id] = (num_intervals == 1)
1498 ? node_split_counts[0]
1499 : node_split_counts[0];
1500
1501 SplayTree node_tree;
1502 int n_buckets = 0;
1503
1504 if (num_intervals == 1) {
1505 // Single-dimensional splitting.
1506 for (int j = 0; j < node_split_counts[0]; ++j) {
1507 auto [start, end] = calculate_interval(
1508 VRPNode.lb[0], VRPNode.ub[0], node_base_interval[0], j,
1509 node_split_counts[0], D == Direction::Forward);
1510 // Clamp to the node's bounds.
1511 if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
1512 start = std::max(start, VRPNode.lb[0]);
1513 } else {
1514 end = std::min(end, VRPNode.ub[0]);
1515 }
1516 buckets.push_back(Bucket(VRPNode.id,
1517 std::vector<double>{start},
1518 std::vector<double>{end}));
1519 node_tree.insert(std::vector<double>{start},
1520 std::vector<double>{end}, bucket_index++);
1521 n_buckets++;
1522 cum_sum++;
1523 }
1524 } else {
1525 // Multi-dimensional splitting.
1526 std::fill(pos.begin(), pos.end(), 0);
1527 while (true) {
1528 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
1529 auto [start, end] = calculate_interval(
1530 VRPNode.lb[r], VRPNode.ub[r], node_base_interval[r],
1531 pos[r], node_split_counts[r],
1532 D == Direction::Forward);
1533 interval_start[r] = start;
1534 interval_end[r] = end;
1535 // Optionally clamp to global resource bounds.
1536 if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
1537 interval_start[r] =
1538 std::max(interval_start[r], R_min[r]);
1539 } else {
1540 interval_end[r] =
1541 std::min(interval_end[r], R_max[r]);
1542 }
1543 }
1544 buckets.push_back(
1545 Bucket(VRPNode.id, interval_start, interval_end));
1546 node_tree.insert(interval_start, interval_end,
1547 bucket_index++);
1548 n_buckets++;
1549 cum_sum++;
1550
1551 // Generate next combination of splits.
1552 int i = 0;
1553 while (i < num_intervals) {
1554 ++pos[i];
1555 if (pos[i] < node_split_counts[i]) {
1556 break;
1557 } else {
1558 pos[i] = 0;
1559 ++i;
1560 }
1561 }
1562 if (i == num_intervals) break;
1563 }
1564 }
1565
1566 // Update per-node bucket bookkeeping.
1567 num_buckets[VRPNode.id] = n_buckets;
1568 num_buckets_index[VRPNode.id] = cum_sum - n_buckets;
1569 node_interval_trees[VRPNode.id] = node_tree;
1570 }
1571
1572 // Update the overall bucket count.
1573 buckets_size = buckets.size();
1574 }

double roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(double value)

◆ define_elementarity_sets()

void BucketGraph::define_elementarity_sets ( )
inline

Definition at line 404 of file BucketGraph.h.

404 {
405 size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
406
407 // Initialize elementarity sets
408 elementarity_bitmap.resize(num_nodes); // Bitmap for elementarity sets
409
410 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
411 // Each customer should appear in its own elementarity
412 // set
413 size_t num_segments = (num_nodes + 63) / 64;
414 elementarity_bitmap[i].resize(num_segments, 0);
415
416 // Mark the node itself in the elementarity set
417 size_t segment_self = i >> 6;
418 size_t bit_position_self = i & 63;
419 elementarity_bitmap[i][segment_self] |= (1ULL << bit_position_self);
420 }
421 }

423 {
424 size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
425
426 // Initialize packing sets
427 packing_bitmap.resize(num_nodes); // Bitmap for packing sets
428
429 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
430 // Each customer should appear in its own packing set
431 size_t num_segments = (num_nodes + 63) / 64;
432 packing_bitmap[i].resize(num_segments, 0);
433
434 // Mark the node itself in the packing set
435 size_t segment_self = i >> 6;
436 size_t bit_position_self = i & 63;
437 packing_bitmap[i][segment_self] |= (1ULL << bit_position_self);
438 }
439 }

◆ DominatedInCompWiseSmallerBuckets() [1/2]

template<Direction D, Stage S>
bool BucketGraph::DominatedInCompWiseSmallerBuckets ( const Label *__restrict__ L,
int bucket,
const std::vector< double > &__restrict__ c_bar,
std::vector< uint64_t > &__restrict__ Bvisited,
int & stat_n_dom )
inlinenoexcept

Determines if a label is dominated in component-wise smaller buckets.

This function checks if a given label is dominated by any other label in component-wise smaller buckets. The dominance is determined based on the cost and order of the buckets.

Definition at line 933 of file BucketSolve.h.

936 {
937 // Get direction-specific data once.
938 const auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
939 const auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
940 const auto &uf = assign_buckets<D>(fw_union_find, bw_union_find);
941 const auto &sccs = assign_buckets<D>(fw_ordered_sccs, bw_ordered_sccs);
942 auto &union_cache = assign_buckets<D>(fw_union_cache, bw_union_cache);
943
944 const auto &topological_order =
945 assign_buckets<D>(fw_topological_order, bw_topological_order);
946
947 // Cache key label properties.
948 const int b_L = L->vertex;
949 const double label_cost = L->cost;
950 const size_t res_size = options.resources.size();
951
952 // Use a thread-local stack to traverse buckets.
953 static thread_local std::vector<int> stack_buffer;
954 stack_buffer.clear(); // Clear previous state.
955 stack_buffer.reserve(R_SIZE);
956 stack_buffer.push_back(bucket);
957
958 // Precompute pointer to bit mask lookup for fast bit operations.
959 const uint64_t *bit_mask_lookup_ptr = bit_mask_lookup.data();
960
961 // Lambda to check if any label in a bucket dominates L.
962 const auto check_dominance = [&](const std::vector<Label *> &labels,
963 int &stat_n_dom) -> bool {
964#ifndef __AVX2__
965 if (labels.size() >= 32) {
966 return check_dominance_against_vector<D, S>(L, labels, cut_storage,
967 res_size, stat_n_dom);
968 } else
969#endif
970 {
971 for (auto *existing_label : labels) {
972 if (!existing_label->is_dominated &&
973 is_dominated<D, S>(L, existing_label)) {
974 ++stat_n_dom;
975 return true;
976 }
977 }
978 return false;
979 }
980 };
981
982 // Main processing loop: traverse buckets using DFS.
983 while (!stack_buffer.empty()) {
984 const int current_bucket = stack_buffer.back();
985 stack_buffer.pop_back();
986
987 // Mark current_bucket as visited using precomputed bit masks.
988 const size_t segment = static_cast<size_t>(current_bucket) >> 6;
989 const uint64_t bit = bit_mask_lookup_ptr[current_bucket & 63];
990 Bvisited[segment] |= bit;
991
992 // Early exit: if label cost is lower than the bucket's c_bar and
993 // the bucket precedes L in the SCC order, then L is not dominated.
994 if (label_cost < c_bar[current_bucket] &&
995 ::precedes(sccs, topological_order, current_bucket, b_L, uf,
996 union_cache)) {
997 return false;
998 }
999
1000 // Check for dominance in this bucket if it's not the label's own
1001 // bucket.
1002 if (b_L != current_bucket) {
1003 const auto &mother_bucket = buckets[current_bucket];
1004 if (mother_bucket.check_dominance(L, check_dominance, stat_n_dom)) {
1005 return true;
1006 }
1007 }
1008
1009 // Process neighbor buckets.
1010 const auto &neighbors = Phi[current_bucket];
1011 for (auto b_prime : neighbors) {
1012 const size_t word_idx = static_cast<size_t>(b_prime) >> 6;
1013 const uint64_t mask = bit_mask_lookup_ptr[b_prime & 63];
1014 if (!(Bvisited[word_idx] & mask)) {
1015 stack_buffer.push_back(b_prime);
1016 }
1017 }
1018 }
1019
1020 return false;
1021}
check_dominance_against_vector
bool check_dominance_against_vector(const Label *new_label, const std::vector< Label * > &labels, const CutStorage *cut_storage, int r_size, int &stat_n_dom) noexcept

template<Direction D, Stage S, ArcType A, Mutability M, Full F>
std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::Extend ( const std::conditional_t< M==Mutability::Mut, Label *, const Label * > L_prime,
const std::conditional_t< A==ArcType::Bucket, BucketArc, std::conditional_t< A==ArcType::Jump, JumpArc, Arc > > & gamma,
int depth )
inlinenoexcept

Extends the label L_prime with the given BucketArc gamma.

Definition at line 590 of file BucketSolve.h.

596 {
597 // Prepare temporary resource storage (resize to current resources size)
598 static thread_local std::vector<double> new_resources;
599 const size_t n_resources = options.resources.size();
600 new_resources.resize(n_resources);
601
602 // Cache initial values from L_prime
603 const int initial_node_id = L_prime->node_id;
604 auto initial_resources = L_prime->resources; // Copy resources from L_prime
605 const double initial_cost = L_prime->cost;
606
607 int node_id;
608 if constexpr (A == ArcType::Bucket) {
609 node_id =
610 assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets)[gamma.to_bucket].node_id;
611 } else if constexpr (A == ArcType::Node) {
612 node_id = gamma.to;
613 } else { // Jump arc
614 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
615 node_id = buckets[gamma.jump_bucket].node_id;
616 const auto &bucket = buckets[gamma.jump_bucket];
617 // Update resource bounds based on jump bucket properties
618 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_resources; ++i) {
619 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
620 initial_resources[i] = std::max(
621 initial_resources[i], static_cast<double>(bucket.lb[i]));
622 } else {
623 initial_resources[i] = std::min(
624 initial_resources[i], static_cast<double>(bucket.ub[i]));
625 }
626 }
627 }
628
629 // Early rejection: if we loop back or the node was already visited.
630 if (node_id == L_prime->node_id ||
631 is_node_visited(L_prime->visited_bitmap, node_id))
632 return {};
633
634 // For stages where fixed arcs matter.
635 if constexpr (S == Stage::Three || S == Stage::Eliminate) {
636 if ((D == Direction::Forward &&
637 is_arc_fixed(initial_node_id, node_id)) ||
638 (D == Direction::Backward &&
639 is_arc_fixed(node_id, initial_node_id)))
640 return {};
641 }
642
643 // Process resources (feasibility check)
644 if constexpr (F != Full::TSP) {
645 if (!process_all_resources<D>(new_resources, initial_resources, gamma,
646 nodes[node_id], n_resources))
647 return {};
648 }
649
650 // For PSTEP/TSP: Check path length constraints.
651 if constexpr (F == Full::PSTEP || F == Full::TSP) {
652 int n_visited = 0;
653 for (const auto &bitmap : L_prime->visited_bitmap)
654 n_visited += __builtin_popcountll(bitmap);
655 if (n_visited > options.max_path_size ||
656 (n_visited == options.max_path_size &&
657 node_id != options.end_depot))
658 return {};
659 }
660
661 const int to_bucket = get_bucket_number<D>(node_id, new_resources);
662
663#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
664 // If bucket is fixed (for Stage::Four) and not a jump arc, try jump arcs
665 // recursively.
666 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four && A != ArcType::Jump) {
667 if (is_bucket_fixed<D>(L_prime->vertex, to_bucket)) {
668 // if (depth > 1) return {};
669 static thread_local std::vector<Label *> label_vector;
670 label_vector.clear();
671 auto jump_arcs =
672 nodes[L_prime->node_id].template get_jump_arcs<D>(node_id);
673 for (const auto &jump_arc : jump_arcs) {
674 auto extended_labels =
675 Extend<D, S, ArcType::Jump, Mutability::Const, F>(
676 L_prime, jump_arc, depth + 1);
677 if (!extended_labels.empty())
678 label_vector.insert(label_vector.end(),
679 extended_labels.begin(),
680 extended_labels.end());
681 }
682 return label_vector;
683 }
684 }
685#endif
686
687#ifdef CUSTOM_COST
688 const auto distance = getcij(initial_node_id, node_id);
689 double new_cost = cost_calculator.calculate_cost(initial_cost, distance,
690 node_id, new_resources);
691#else
692 double new_cost = initial_cost + getcij(initial_node_id, node_id);
693#endif
694
695 const auto &VRPNode = nodes[node_id];
696 if constexpr (F != Full::PSTEP) {
697 new_cost -= VRPNode.cost;
698 } else {
699 int n_visited = 0;
700 for (const auto &bitmap : L_prime->visited_bitmap)
701 n_visited += __builtin_popcountll(bitmap);
702 if (n_visited > 1 && initial_node_id != options.depot)
703 new_cost += pstep_duals.getThreeTwoDualValue(initial_node_id) +
704 pstep_duals.getThreeThreeDualValue(initial_node_id);
705 new_cost += -pstep_duals.getThreeTwoDualValue(node_id) +
706 pstep_duals.getArcDualValue(initial_node_id, node_id);
707 }
708
709 // Apply branching dual adjustments if present.
710 if (!branching_duals->empty()) {
711 new_cost += (D == Direction::Forward)
712 ? branching_duals->getDual(initial_node_id, node_id)
713 : branching_duals->getDual(node_id, initial_node_id);
714 new_cost += branching_duals->getDual(node_id);
715 }
716
717 RCC_MODE_BLOCK(if constexpr (S == Stage::Four) {
718 new_cost -= (D == Direction::Forward)
719 ? arc_duals.getDual(initial_node_id, node_id)
720 : arc_duals.getDual(node_id, initial_node_id);
721 })
722
723 // Handle Reverse mode early.
724 if constexpr (F == Full::Reverse) {
725 if (new_resources[options.main_resources[0]] <=
726 std::floor(q_star[options.main_resources[0]]))
727 return {};
728 // auto &label_pool = assign_buckets<D>(label_pool_fw, label_pool_bw);
729 auto new_label = new Label();
730 if (to_bucket == -1) {
731 fmt::print("Warning: to_bucket is -1\n");
732 }
733 new_label->vertex = to_bucket;
734 return {new_label};
735 }
736
737 // Create and initialize a new label.
738 auto &label_pool = assign_buckets<D>(label_pool_fw, label_pool_bw);
739 auto new_label = label_pool->acquire();
740 new_label->initialize(to_bucket, new_cost, new_resources, node_id);
741 new_label->vertex = to_bucket;
742 new_label->real_cost =
743 L_prime->real_cost + getcij(initial_node_id, node_id);
744
745 if constexpr (M == Mutability::Mut) L_prime->children.push_back(new_label);
746
747 // Update visited bitmap: intersect parent's visited bits with neighborhood
748 // mask.
749 if constexpr (F != Full::PSTEP) {
750 for (size_t i = 0; i < new_label->visited_bitmap.size(); ++i) {
751 const uint64_t current_visited = L_prime->visited_bitmap[i];
752 if (current_visited)
753 new_label->visited_bitmap[i] =
754 current_visited & neighborhoods_bitmap[node_id][i];
755 }
756 } else {
757 new_label->visited_bitmap = L_prime->visited_bitmap;
758 }
759 set_node_visited(new_label->visited_bitmap, node_id);
760
761 // Update the path (nodes covered) by copying parent's path and adding
762 // current node.
763 new_label->nodes_covered = L_prime->nodes_covered;
764 new_label->nodes_covered.push_back(node_id);
765
766#if defined(SRC)
767 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four || S == Stage::Enumerate) {
768 new_label->SRCmap = L_prime->SRCmap;
769 auto &cutter = cut_storage;
770 const auto active_cuts = cutter->getActiveCuts();
771 const auto n_cuts = cutter->activeSize();
772
773 if (n_cuts == 0) return {new_label};
774
775 double total_cost_update = 0.0;
776 const size_t segment = node_id >> 6;
777 const size_t bit_position = node_id & 63;
778
779 auto &masks = cutter->getSegmentMasks();
780 const auto cut_limit_mask = masks.get_cut_limit_mask();
781 uint64_t valid_cuts = masks.get_valid_cut_mask(segment, bit_position);
782 valid_cuts &= cut_limit_mask; // Safeguard
783
784 while (valid_cuts) {
785 const int cut_idx = __builtin_ctzll(valid_cuts);
786 const auto &active_cut = active_cuts[cut_idx];
787 const auto &cut = *active_cut.cut_ptr;
788 auto &src_map_value = new_label->SRCmap[active_cut.index];
789 src_map_value += cut.p.num[cut.baseSetOrder[node_id]];
790 if (src_map_value >= cut.p.den) {
791 src_map_value -= cut.p.den;
792 total_cost_update -= active_cut.dual_value;
793 }
794 valid_cuts &= (valid_cuts - 1);
795 }
796
797 uint64_t to_clear =
798 (~masks.get_neighbor_mask(segment, bit_position)) & cut_limit_mask;
799 while (to_clear) {
800 const int cut_idx = __builtin_ctzll(to_clear);
801 if (cut_idx >= n_cuts) {
802 fmt::print("Warning: Invalid clear_idx {} >= n_cuts {}\n",
803 cut_idx, n_cuts);
804 break;
805 }
806 new_label->SRCmap[active_cuts[cut_idx].index] = 0.0;
807 to_clear &= (to_clear - 1);
808 }
809 new_label->cost += total_cost_update;
810 }
811#endif
812
813 return std::vector<Label *>{new_label};
814}

Definition BucketGraph.h:299

◆ forbidCycle()

void BucketGraph::forbidCycle ( const std::vector< int > & cycle,
bool aggressive )

Forbids a cycle in the bucket graph.

This function takes a vector representing a cycle in the graph and forbids the edges corresponding to the cycle. If the 'aggressive' flag is set to true, it also forbids additional edges between the vertices of the cycle.

Definition at line 438 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

438 {
439 // If the cycle is too short, there is nothing to forbid.
440 if (cycle.size() < 2) return;
441
442 // Process each adjacent pair in the cycle.
443 for (size_t i = 0; i < cycle.size() - 1; ++i) {
444 int v1 = cycle[i];
445 int v2 = cycle[i + 1];
446
447 // Forbid v2 in the neighborhood of v1.
448 size_t segment = static_cast<size_t>(v2) >> 6;
449 size_t bit_position = static_cast<size_t>(v2) & 63;
450 neighborhoods_bitmap[v1][segment] |= (1ULL << bit_position);
451
452 if (aggressive) {
453 // Additionally, forbid v1 in the neighborhood of v2.
454 segment = static_cast<size_t>(v1) >> 6;
455 bit_position = static_cast<size_t>(v1) & 63;
456 neighborhoods_bitmap[v2][segment] |= (1ULL << bit_position);
457 }
458 }
459}

◆ generate_arcs() [1/2]

void BucketGraph::generate_arcs ( )

Generates arcs in both forward and backward directions in parallel.

This function uses OpenMP to parallelize the generation of arcs in both forward and backward directions. It performs the following steps for each direction:

  • Calls the generate_arcs function template with the appropriate direction.
  • Clears and resizes the Phi vector for the respective direction.
  • Computes the Phi values for each bucket and stores them in the Phi vector.
  • Calls the SCC_handler function template with the appropriate direction.

The forward direction operations are performed in one OpenMP section, and the backward direction operations are performed in another OpenMP section.

Definition at line 800 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

800 {
801 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
802 work, bi_sched,
803 SECTION {
804 // Task for Forward Direction
805 generate_arcs<Direction::Forward>();
806 Phi_fw.clear();
807 Phi_fw.resize(fw_buckets_size);
808 for (int i = 0; i < fw_buckets_size; ++i) {
809 Phi_fw[i] = computePhi(i, true);
810 }
811 SCC_handler<Direction::Forward>();
812 },
813 SECTION {
814 // Task for Backward Direction
815 generate_arcs<Direction::Backward>();
816 Phi_bw.clear();
817 Phi_bw.resize(bw_buckets_size);
818 for (int i = 0; i < bw_buckets_size; ++i) {
819 Phi_bw[i] = computePhi(i, false);
820 }
821 SCC_handler<Direction::Backward>();
822 });
823}

void SCC_handler()
Handles the computation of Strongly Connected Components (SCCs) for the BucketGraph.
