◆ generate_arcs() [2/2]

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::generate_arcs ( )

Generates arcs in the bucket graph based on the specified direction.

Generates arcs in the bucket graph based on the specified direction.

Definition at line 429 of file BucketUtils.h.

429 {
430 // Mutex for protecting global bucket updates.
431 std::mutex buckets_mutex;
432
433 // Clear the appropriate graph.
434 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
435 fw_bucket_graph.clear();
436 } else {
437 bw_bucket_graph.clear();
438 }
439
440 // Resolve references based on the direction.
441 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
442 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
443 auto &num_buckets_idx =
444 assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
445 // Note: we no longer rely solely on bucket_splits here.
446
447 // Pre-compute intervals (base widths) for each node and each resource,
448 // using the same logic as in define_buckets.
449 const int num_resources = options.resources.size();
450 std::vector<std::vector<double>> node_base_intervals(nodes.size());
451 // Also store the computed splits per resource for each node.
452 std::vector<std::vector<int>> node_split_counts(nodes.size());
453
454 for (size_t node_id = 0; node_id < nodes.size(); ++node_id) {
455 const auto &node = nodes[node_id];
456 node_base_intervals[node_id].resize(num_resources);
457 node_split_counts[node_id].resize(num_resources);
458 for (int r = 0; r < num_resources; ++r) {
459 double full_range = R_max[r] - R_min[r];
460 double node_range = node.ub[r] - node.lb[r];
461 int splits = 1;
462 if (std::fabs(full_range) >
463 std::numeric_limits<double>::epsilon()) {
464 double splits_d =
465 (node_range * intervals[r].interval) / full_range;
466 splits = std::max(1, static_cast<int>(std::round(splits_d)));
467 }
468 node_split_counts[node_id][r] = splits;
469 node_base_intervals[node_id][r] =
470 (node.ub[r] - node.lb[r]) / static_cast<double>(splits);
471 }
472 }
473
474 // Clear all buckets and their arcs.
475 for (auto &bucket : buckets) {
476 bucket.clear();
477 bucket.clear_arcs(D == Direction::Forward);
478 }
479
480 // Helper lambda: validate an arc and, if valid, add it.
481 auto try_add_arc =
482 [&](const VRPNode &curr_node, int from_bucket, const VRPNode &next_node,
483 int to_bucket, const std::vector<double> &curr_node_base,
484 const std::vector<double> &next_node_base,
485 const std::vector<double> &res_inc, double cost_inc) -> bool {
486 bool valid = true;
487 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
488 for (int r = 0; r < res_inc.size() && valid; ++r) {
489 // For forward, check that adding the resource increment from
490 // the from-bucket does not push us past the next node's upper
491 // bound.
492 if (buckets[from_bucket].lb[r] + res_inc[r] > next_node.ub[r]) {
493 valid = false;
494 } else {
495 double max_calc =
496 std::max(buckets[from_bucket].lb[r] + res_inc[r],
497 next_node.lb[r]);
498 if (max_calc < buckets[to_bucket].lb[r] ||
499 max_calc >=
500 buckets[to_bucket].lb[r] + next_node_base[r]) {
501 valid = false;
502 }
503 }
504 }
505 } else {
506 for (int r = 0; r < res_inc.size() && valid; ++r) {
507 // For backward, check that subtracting the resource increment
508 // does not drop below next node's lb.
509 if (buckets[from_bucket].ub[r] - res_inc[r] < next_node.lb[r]) {
510 valid = false;
511 } else {
512 double min_calc =
513 std::min(buckets[from_bucket].ub[r] - res_inc[r],
514 next_node.ub[r]);
515 if (min_calc > buckets[to_bucket].ub[r] ||
516 min_calc <=
517 buckets[to_bucket].ub[r] - next_node_base[r]) {
518 valid = false;
519 }
520 }
521 }
522 }
523 if (valid) {
524 // Add the arc to the global structure.
525 {
526 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(buckets_mutex);
527 add_arc<D>(from_bucket, to_bucket, res_inc, cost_inc);
528 buckets[from_bucket].template add_bucket_arc<D>(
529 from_bucket, to_bucket, res_inc, cost_inc, false);
530 }
531 }
532 return valid;
533 };
534
535 // Lambda to process a single node.
536 auto process_node = [&](int node_id) {
537 const auto &node = nodes[node_id];
538 std::vector<double> res_inc(num_resources);
539 // Local container for arcs (if further processing is needed).
540 std::vector<std::pair<int, int>> local_arcs;
541 local_arcs.reserve(100); // heuristic reserve
542
543 const auto arcs = node.get_arcs<D>();
544 // Use the pre-computed base intervals for the current node.
545 const auto &curr_node_base = node_base_intervals[node.id];
546
547 // For every bucket in the current node.
548 for (int i = 0; i < num_buckets[node.id]; ++i) {
549 int from_bucket = i + num_buckets_idx[node.id];
550 // Process each outgoing arc.
551 for (const auto &arc : arcs) {
552 // Avoid self-loop.
553 if (node.id == nodes[arc.to].id) continue;
554
555 const auto &next_node = nodes[arc.to];
556 const auto &next_node_base = node_base_intervals[arc.to];
557 const double travel_cost = getcij(node.id, next_node.id);
558 const double cost_inc = travel_cost - next_node.cost;
559
560 // Pre-calculate resource increments.
561 for (int r = 0; r < num_resources; r++) {
562 res_inc[r] = node.consumption[r];
563 if (options.resources[r] == "time") {
564 res_inc[r] += travel_cost;
565 }
566 }
567
568 // Iterate over buckets in the next node.
569 for (int j = 0; j < num_buckets[next_node.id]; ++j) {
570 int to_bucket = j + num_buckets_idx[next_node.id];
571 if (from_bucket == to_bucket ||
572 is_bucket_fixed<D>(from_bucket, to_bucket)) {
573 continue;
574 }
575 // Validate and add arc if valid.
576 if (try_add_arc(node, from_bucket, next_node, to_bucket,
577 curr_node_base, next_node_base, res_inc,
578 cost_inc)) {
579 local_arcs.emplace_back(from_bucket, to_bucket);
580 }
581 }
582 }
583 }
584 // If needed, local_arcs can be used for debugging or further
585 // processing.
586 };
587
588 // Create a list of tasks (each task is a node index).
589 std::vector<int> tasks(nodes.size());
590 std::iota(tasks.begin(), tasks.end(), 0);
591
592 // Choose a chunk size based on a heuristic.
593 constexpr int chunk_size = 10;
594
595 // Set up the thread pool.
596 const unsigned int thread_count =
597 std::max(1u, std::thread::hardware_concurrency() / 2);
598 exec::static_thread_pool pool(thread_count);
599 auto sched = pool.get_scheduler();
600
601 // Launch tasks in parallel over chunks.
602 auto bulk_sender = stdexec::bulk(
603 stdexec::just(), (tasks.size() + chunk_size - 1) / chunk_size,
604 [&, chunk_size](std::size_t chunk_idx) {
605 const size_t start_idx = chunk_idx * chunk_size;
606 const size_t end_idx =
607 std::min(start_idx + chunk_size, tasks.size());
608 for (size_t idx = start_idx; idx < end_idx; ++idx) {
609 process_node(tasks[idx]);
610 }
611 });
612
613 auto work = stdexec::starts_on(sched, bulk_sender);
614 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));
615}
pool
thread_local std::vector< std::vector< T > > ThreadLocalPool< T >::pool

template<Symmetry SYM>
AdjList BucketGraph::get_adjacency_list ( )
inline

Definition at line 1300 of file BucketGraph.h.

1300 {
1301 AdjList adjacency_list;
1302
1303 // Iterate through all nodes in the graph
1304 for (const auto &node : nodes) {
1305 std::vector<std::tuple<int, double, int>> arcs;
1306
1307 // Retrieve all arcs associated with the current node
1308 for (const auto &arc : node.get_arcs<Direction::Forward>()) {
1309 int to_node = arc.to;
1310 double cost = getcij(node.id, to_node) - nodes[to_node].cost;
1311 int capacity = 1;
1312
1313 // Only add arcs with non-zero capacity
1314 if (capacity > 0) {
1315 arcs.emplace_back(to_node, cost, capacity);
1316 }
1317 }
1318
1319 // Add the node's arcs to the adjacency list if it has
1320 // any outgoing edges
1321 if (!arcs.empty()) {
1322 adjacency_list[node.id] = arcs;
1323 }
1324 }
1325
1326 return adjacency_list;
1327 }

◆ get_best_label()

template<Direction D>
Label * BucketGraph::get_best_label ( const std::vector< int > & topological_order,
const std::vector< double > & c_bar,
const std::vector< std::vector< int > > & sccs )

Retrieves the best label from the bucket graph based on the given topological order, c_bar values, and strongly connected components.

This function iterates through the given topological order and for each component, it retrieves the labels from the corresponding buckets in the bucket graph. It then compares the cost of each label and keeps track of the label with the lowest cost. The best label, along with its associated bucket, is returned.

Definition at line 629 of file BucketUtils.h.

631 {
632 double best_cost = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();
633 Label *best_label = nullptr;
634 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
635
636 // Iterate over all buckets, and select the label with the lowest cost.
637 for (auto &bucket : buckets) {
638 Label *label = bucket.get_best_label();
639 if (!label) continue;
640 if (label->cost < best_cost) {
641 best_cost = label->cost;
642 best_label = label;
643 }
644 }
645 return best_label;
646}

template<Direction D>
int BucketGraph::get_bucket_number ( int node,
std::vector< double > & values )
inlinenoexcept

Definition at line 86 of file BucketUtils.h.

87 {
88 const size_t num_resources = options.main_resources.size();
89
90 const auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
91
92 // Optionally adjust resource values (uncomment if you need epsilon
93 // adjustments)
94 for (size_t r = 0; r < num_resources; ++r) {
95 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
96 // resource_values_vec[r] += numericutils::eps;
97 } else { // Direction::Backward
98 // resource_values_vec[r] -= numericutils::eps;
99 }
100 }
101 auto &num_buckets_index =
102 assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
103 // Start with the offset for this node’s buckets.
104 int bucket_number = num_buckets_index[node];
105 int multiplier = 1;
106
107 for (size_t r = 0; r < num_resources; ++r) {
108 // Node-specific resource bounds.
109 double node_lb = nodes[node].lb[r];
110 double node_ub = nodes[node].ub[r];
111
112 // Global bounds (if needed for computing splits).
113 double global_lb = R_min[r];
114 double global_ub = R_max[r];
115 double full_range = global_ub - global_lb;
116 double node_range = node_ub - node_lb;
117
118 // Determine the number of splits (i.e. intervals) for resource r.
119 int splits =
120 std::max(1, static_cast<int>(std::round((node_range / full_range) *
121 intervals[r].interval)));
122 double interval_width = node_range / splits;
123
124 int pos = 0;
125 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
126 // Compute the bucket "digit" from the lower bound.
127 pos = std::min(
128 splits - 1,
129 static_cast<int>(std::floor((resource_values_vec[r] - node_lb) /
130 interval_width)));
131 } else { // Direction::Backward
132 // For backward, we consider the distance from the upper bound.
133 pos = std::min(
134 splits - 1,
135 static_cast<int>(std::floor((node_ub - resource_values_vec[r]) /
136 interval_width)));
137 }
138
139 bucket_number += pos * multiplier;
140 multiplier *= splits;
141 }
142
143 // check if buckets[bucket_number] contains the resource_vec
144 if (!buckets[bucket_number].contains(resource_values_vec)) {
145 std::throw_with_nested(
146 std::runtime_error("Resource values not contained in bucket"));
147 return -1;
148 }
149
150 return bucket_number;
151}

◆ get_opposite_bucket_number()

template<Direction D>
int BucketGraph::get_opposite_bucket_number ( int current_bucket_index,
std::vector< double > & inc )

Get the opposite bucket number for a given bucket index.

This function retrieves the opposite bucket number based on the current bucket index and the specified direction. It determines the node and bounds of the current bucket, then calculates the opposite bucket index using the appropriate direction.

Definition at line 988 of file BucketUtils.h.

989 {
990 // Retrieve the current bucket based on the current direction.
991 const auto &current_bucket = (D == Direction::Forward)
992 ? fw_buckets[current_bucket_index]
993 : bw_buckets[current_bucket_index];
994 const int node = current_bucket.node_id;
995 const auto &theNode = nodes[node];
996
997 // Pre-compute the reference point for the given node using the main
998 // resources.
999 const size_t num_resources = options.main_resources.size();
1000 // Optionally, add an assertion or check that inc has the expected size.
1001 assert(inc.size() == num_resources && theNode.lb.size() == num_resources &&
1002 theNode.ub.size() == num_resources);
1003
1004 std::vector<double> reference_point(num_resources);
1005
1006 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
1007 // For Forward direction:
1008 // Use the maximum of inc and the node's lower bounds.
1009 // This ensures we do not fall below the allowed lower bound.
1010 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_resources; ++i) {
1011 reference_point[i] = std::max(inc[i], theNode.lb[i]);
1012 // Optionally, adjust with epsilon if needed:
1013 // reference_point[i] += numericutils::eps;
1014 }
1015 // Get the opposite bucket using the Backward tree.
1016 return get_bucket_number<Direction::Backward>(node, reference_point);
1017 } else {
1018 // For Backward direction:
1019 // Use the minimum of inc and the node's upper bounds.
1020 // This ensures we do not exceed the allowed upper bound.
1021 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_resources; ++i) {
1022 reference_point[i] = std::min(inc[i], theNode.ub[i]);
1023 // Optionally, adjust with epsilon if needed:
1024 // reference_point[i] -= numericutils::eps;
1025 }
1026 // Get the opposite bucket using the Forward tree.
1027 return get_bucket_number<Direction::Forward>(node, reference_point);
1028 }
1029}

◆ heuristic_fixing()

template<Stage S>
void BucketGraph::heuristic_fixing ( )

Applies heuristic fixing to the current solution.

This function modifies the current solution based on the heuristic fixing strategy using the provided vector of values.

Definition at line 1111 of file BucketUtils.h.

1111 {
1112 // Reset and initialize state.
1113 reset_pool();
1114 reset_fixed();
1115 common_initialization();
1116
1117 // Pre-allocate c-bar vectors with infinity.
1118 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(fw_buckets.size(),
1119 std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
1120 std::vector<double> backward_cbar(bw_buckets.size(),
1121 std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
1122
1123 // Run the labeling algorithms for Stage::One with Full::Partial mode.
1124 run_labeling_algorithms<Stage::One, Full::Partial>(forward_cbar,
1125 backward_cbar);
1126
1127 // Prepare containers for grouping labels by node.
1128 const size_t num_nodes = nodes.size();
1129 std::vector<std::vector<Label *>> fw_labels_map(num_nodes);
1130 std::vector<std::vector<Label *>> bw_labels_map(num_nodes);
1131
1132 // Estimate average bucket size to pre-reserve capacity.
1133 size_t avg_bucket_size = 0;
1134 for (const auto &bucket : fw_buckets) {
1135 avg_bucket_size += bucket.get_labels().size();
1136 }
1137 avg_bucket_size /= (fw_buckets.empty() ? 1 : fw_buckets.size());
1138 for (auto &labels : fw_labels_map) labels.reserve(avg_bucket_size);
1139 for (auto &labels : bw_labels_map) labels.reserve(avg_bucket_size);
1140
1141 // Helper lambda to group labels from buckets into a node-indexed map.
1142 auto group_labels = [](const auto &buckets, auto &labels_map) {
1143 for (const auto &bucket : buckets) {
1144 for (auto *label : bucket.get_labels()) {
1145 labels_map[label->node_id].push_back(label);
1146 }
1147 }
1148 };
1149
1150 // Parallel grouping of forward and backward labels.
1151 auto forward_task = stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() {
1152 group_labels(fw_buckets, fw_labels_map);
1153 });
1154 auto backward_task = stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() {
1155 group_labels(bw_buckets, bw_labels_map);
1156 });
1157 stdexec::sync_wait(
1158 stdexec::when_all(std::move(forward_task), std::move(backward_task)));
1159
1160 // Pre-compute the minimum cost label for each node.
1161 std::vector<const Label *> min_fw_labels(num_nodes, nullptr);
1162 std::vector<const Label *> min_bw_labels(num_nodes, nullptr);
1163 auto compute_min_labels = [](const auto &labels_map, auto &min_labels) {
1164 for (size_t i = 0; i < labels_map.size(); ++i) {
1165 if (!labels_map[i].empty()) {
1166 min_labels[i] = *std::min_element(
1167 labels_map[i].begin(), labels_map[i].end(),
1168 [](const Label *a, const Label *b) {
1169 return a->cost < b->cost;
1170 });
1171 }
1172 }
1173 };
1174
1175 // Parallel computation of minimum labels.
1176 auto min_fw_task = stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() {
1177 compute_min_labels(fw_labels_map, min_fw_labels);
1178 });
1179 auto min_bw_task = stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() {
1180 compute_min_labels(bw_labels_map, min_bw_labels);
1181 });
1182 stdexec::sync_wait(
1183 stdexec::when_all(std::move(min_fw_task), std::move(min_bw_task)));
1184
1185 // Pre-compute the index of the "time" resource.
1186 const size_t time_index = std::distance(
1187 options.resources.begin(),
1188 std::find(options.resources.begin(), options.resources.end(), "time"));
1189
1190 size_t num_fixes = 0;
1191 // Iterate over all node pairs to check for fixable arcs.
1192 for (size_t i = 0; i < num_nodes; ++i) {
1193 const auto *min_fw_label = min_fw_labels[i];
1194 if (!min_fw_label) continue;
1195
1196 const VRPNode &L_last_node = nodes[min_fw_label->node_id];
1197
1198 for (size_t j = 0; j < num_nodes; ++j) {
1199 if (i == j) continue;
1200 const auto *min_bw_label = min_bw_labels[j];
1201 if (!min_bw_label) continue;
1202
1203 const double cost =
1204 getcij(min_fw_label->node_id, min_bw_label->node_id);
1205
1206 // Check the "time" resource first (often the most restrictive).
1207 if (options.resources[time_index] == "time") {
1208 if (min_fw_label->resources[time_index] + cost +
1209 L_last_node.duration >
1210 min_bw_label->resources[time_index]) {
1211 continue;
1212 }
1213 }
1214
1215 // Check all other resources.
1216 bool violated = false;
1217 for (size_t r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
1218 if (r != time_index) {
1219 if (min_fw_label->resources[r] +
1220 L_last_node.consumption[r] >
1221 min_bw_label->resources[r]) {
1222 violated = true;
1223 break;
1224 }
1225 }
1226 }
1227
1228 // If all resource constraints are satisfied and the cost exceeds
1229 // the gap, fix the arc.
1230 if (!violated &&
1231 (min_fw_label->cost + cost + min_bw_label->cost > gap)) {
1232 fix_arc(i, j);
1233 ++num_fixes;
1234 }
1235 }
1236 }
1237}
BucketGraph::fix_arc
bool fix_arc(int from, int to) noexcept

VRPNode::consumption

Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ initInfo()

void BucketGraph::initInfo ( )
static

Prints the configuration information of the BucketGraph.

This function outputs the configuration details including resource size, number of clients, and maximum SRC cuts. It also conditionally prints whether RIH, RCC, and SRC are enabled or disabled based on the preprocessor directives.

The output format is as follows:

+-------------------------------—+ | CONFIGURATION INFO | +-------------------------------—+ Resources: <R_SIZE> Number of Clients: <N_SIZE> Maximum SRC cuts: <MAX_SRC_CUTS> RIH: <enabled/disabled> RCC: <enabled/disabled> SRC: <enabled/disabled> +-------------------------------—+

Definition at line 905 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

905 {
906 // Print header
907 fmt::print("\n+----------------------------------+\n");
908 fmt::print("| CONFIGURATION INFO |\n");
909 fmt::print("+----------------------------------+\n");
910
911 // Print Resource size
912 fmt::print("Resources: {}\n", R_SIZE);
913 fmt::print("Number of Clients: {}\n", N_SIZE - 2);
914
915 // Conditional configuration (RIH enabled/disabled)
916#ifdef RIH
917 fmt::print("RIH: enabled\n");
918#else
919 fmt::print("RIH: disabled\n");
920#endif
921#ifdef RCC
922 fmt::print("RCC: enabled\n");
923#else
924 fmt::print("RCC: disabled\n");
925#endif
926#ifdef SRC
927 fmt::print("SRC: enabled\n");
928#else
929 fmt::print("SRC: disabled\n");
930#endif
931 fmt::print("+----------------------------------+\n");
932
933 fmt::print("\n");
934}

◆ is_dominated() [1/2]

template<Direction D, Stage S>
bool BucketGraph::is_dominated ( const Label *__restrict new_label,
const Label *__restrict label )
inlinenoexcept

Checks if a label is dominated by a new label based on cost and resource conditions.

Definition at line 822 of file BucketSolve.h.

823 {
824 constexpr double TOLERANCE = 1e-3;
825
826 // A label cannot dominate if its cost is higher.
827 const double cost_diff = label->cost - new_label->cost;
828 if (cost_diff > TOLERANCE) {
829 return false;
830 }
831
832 // Compare resource vectors.
833 const auto *__restrict new_res = new_label->resources.data();
834 const auto *__restrict lbl_res = label->resources.data();
835 const size_t n_res = options.resources.size();
836
837 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
838 // In the Forward direction, each resource value of 'label' must not
839 // exceed that of 'new_label'
840 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_res; ++i) {
841 if (lbl_res[i] > new_res[i]) {
842 return false;
843 }
844 }
845 } else if constexpr (D == Direction::Backward) {
846 // In the Backward direction, each resource value of 'label' must not be
847 // less than that of 'new_label'
848 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_res; ++i) {
849 if (lbl_res[i] < new_res[i]) {
850 return false;
851 }
852 }
853 }
854
855 // visits of label.
856 if constexpr (S == Stage::Three || S == Stage::Four ||
857 S == Stage::Enumerate) {
858 const size_t n_bitmap = label->visited_bitmap.size();
859 for (size_t i = 0; i < n_bitmap; ++i) {
860 // Every visited node in 'label' must also be visited in
861 // 'new_label'.
862 if ((label->visited_bitmap[i] & new_label->visited_bitmap[i]) !=
863 label->visited_bitmap[i]) {
864 return false;
865 }
866 }
867 }
868
869#ifdef SRC
870 // For Stage Four or Enumerate, apply additional SRC-based cost adjustments.
871 if constexpr (S == Stage::Four || S == Stage::Enumerate) {
872 const auto *__restrict lbl_srcs = label->SRCmap.data();
873 const auto *__restrict new_lbl_srcs = new_label->SRCmap.data();
874 const auto &active_cuts = cut_storage->getActiveCuts();
875 double dual_sum = 0.0;
876
877 // Accumulate the dual values from active cuts if label's SRC exceeds
878 // new_label's.
879 for (const auto &cut : active_cuts) {
880 if (lbl_srcs[cut.index] > new_lbl_srcs[cut.index]) {
881 dual_sum += cut.dual_value;
882 // If the cost difference minus the accumulated duals is still
883 // positive, new_label is not sufficiently dominating.
884 if (cost_diff - dual_sum > TOLERANCE) {
885 return false;
886 }
887 }
888 }
889 }
890#endif
891
892 return true;
893}

◆ is_node_unreachable()

static bool BucketGraph::is_node_unreachable ( const std::array< uint64_t, num_words > & bitmap,
int node_id )
inlinestatic

Checks if a node is unreachable based on a bitmap.

This function determines if a node, identified by its node_id, is unreachable by checking a specific bit in a bitmap. The bitmap is represented as an array of 64-bit unsigned integers.

Definition at line 590 of file BucketGraph.h.

591 {
592 int word_index = node_id >> 6; // Determine which 64-bit segment
593 // contains the node_id
594 int bit_position =
595 node_id & 63; // Determine the bit position within that segment
596 return (bitmap[word_index] & (1ULL << bit_position)) != 0;
597 }

◆ is_node_visited()

static bool BucketGraph::is_node_visited ( const std::array< uint64_t, num_words > & bitmap,
int node_id )
inlinestatic

Checks if a node has been visited based on a bitmap.

This function determines if a specific node, identified by node_id, has been visited by checking the corresponding bit in a bitmap array. The bitmap is an array of 64-bit unsigned integers, where each bit represents the visited status of a node.

Definition at line 555 of file BucketGraph.h.

556 {
557 int word_index = node_id >> 6; // Determine which 64-bit segment
558 // contains the node_id
559 int bit_position =
560 node_id & 63; // Determine the bit position within that segment
561 return (bitmap[word_index] & (1ULL << bit_position)) != 0;
562 }

template<Direction D, Stage S, Full F>
std::vector< double > BucketGraph::labeling_algorithm ( )

Performs the labeling algorithm on the BucketGraph.

206 {
207 // Get references to internal bucket structures to avoid repeated lookups.
208 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
209 const auto &ordered_sccs =
210 assign_buckets<D>(fw_ordered_sccs, bw_ordered_sccs);
211 const auto &topological_order =
212 assign_buckets<D>(fw_topological_order, bw_topological_order);
213 const auto &sccs = assign_buckets<D>(fw_sccs, bw_sccs);
214 const auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
215 auto &c_bar = assign_buckets<D>(fw_c_bar, bw_c_bar);
216 auto &n_labels = assign_buckets<D>(n_fw_labels, n_bw_labels);
217 const auto &sorted_sccs = assign_buckets<D>(fw_sccs_sorted, bw_sccs_sorted);
218 const auto &n_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
219 auto &stat_n_labels = assign_buckets<D>(stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw);
220 auto &stat_n_dom = assign_buckets<D>(stat_n_dom_fw, stat_n_dom_bw);
221 auto &arc_scores = assign_buckets<D>(fw_arc_scores, bw_arc_scores);
222 auto &best_label = assign_buckets<D>(fw_best_label, bw_best_label);
223
224 // Reset the total label count.
225 n_labels = 0;
226
227 // Pre-calculate bitmap segments for dominance-checking.
228 const size_t n_segments = (n_buckets + 63) >> 6;
229 std::vector<uint64_t> Bvisited(n_segments, 0);
230
231 // Process each strongly connected component (SCC) in topological order.
232 for (const auto &scc_index : topological_order) {
233 bool all_ext = false;
234 do {
235 all_ext = true; // Assume all labels are extended unless a new
236 // extension is made.
237
238 // Process each bucket in sorted order within the current SCC.
239 const auto &scc_buckets = sorted_sccs[scc_index];
240 for (const auto bucket : scc_buckets) {
241 // Retrieve all labels from the current bucket.
242 const auto &bucket_labels = buckets[bucket].get_active_labels();
243
244 // Process each label in this bucket.
245 for (Label *label : bucket_labels) {
246 // Skip labels that are already extended or dominated.
247 // if (label->is_extended || label->is_dominated) continue;
248
249 // For partial solutions (if not PSTEP or TSP), check
250 // resource feasibility.
251 if constexpr (F != Full::PSTEP && F != Full::TSP) {
252 if constexpr (F == Full::Partial) {
253 const auto main_resource =
254 label->resources[options.main_resources[0]];
255 const auto q_star_value =
256 std::floor(q_star[options.main_resources[0]]);
257 if ((D == Direction::Forward &&
258 main_resource > q_star_value) ||
259 (D == Direction::Backward &&
260 main_resource <= q_star_value)) {
261 label->set_extended(true);
262 continue;
263 }
264 }
265 }
266
267 // Clear Bvisited bitmap for this label's dominance check.
268 if (n_segments <= 8)
269 std::fill(Bvisited.begin(), Bvisited.end(), 0);
270 else
271 std::memset(Bvisited.data(), 0,
272 n_segments * sizeof(uint64_t));
273
274 // Check if this label is dominated by labels in smaller
275 // buckets.
276 if constexpr (F != Full::TSP) {
277 // if constexpr (S > Stage::Three) {
278 if (DominatedInCompWiseSmallerBuckets<D, S>(
279 label, bucket, c_bar, Bvisited, stat_n_dom)) {
280 // label->set_dominated(true);
281 continue;
282 }
283 // }
284 }
285
286 // Process outgoing arcs for the current label.
287 const int node_id = label->node_id;
288 const auto &node_arcs =
289 nodes[node_id].template get_arcs<D>();
290
291 for (const auto &arc : node_arcs) {
292 // Extend the label along the arc.
293 auto extended_labels =
294 Extend<D, S, ArcType::Node, Mutability::Mut, F>(
295 label, arc);
296 if (extended_labels.empty()) {
297#ifdef UNREACHABLE_DOMINANCE
298 set_node_unreachable(label->unreachable_bitmap,
299 arc.to);
300#endif
301 continue;
302 }
303 // Update arc score with the number of extended labels.
304 arc_scores[node_id][arc] += extended_labels.size();
305
306 // Process each new label produced by the extension.
307 for (Label *new_label : extended_labels) {
308 ++stat_n_labels;
309 const int to_bucket = new_label->vertex;
310 auto &mother_bucket = buckets[to_bucket];
311 const auto &to_bucket_labels =
312 mother_bucket.get_non_dominated_labels();
313
314 bool dominated = false;
315 if constexpr (S == Stage::One) {
316 // In Stage One, use an early exit: mark labels
317 // with higher cost as dominated.
318 for (auto *existing_label : to_bucket_labels) {
319 if (label->cost < existing_label->cost)
320 existing_label->set_dominated(true);
321 else {
322 dominated = true;
323 break;
324 }
325 }
326 } else {
327 // For later stages, use a SIMD-friendly
328 // dominance check.
329 auto check_dominance_in_bucket =
330 [&](const std::vector<Label *> &labels,
331 int &stat_n_dom) {
332#ifndef __AVX2__
333 if (labels.size() >= 32)
334 return check_dominance_against_vector<
335 D, S>(new_label, labels,
336 cut_storage,
337 options.resources.size(),
338 stat_n_dom);
339 else
340#endif
341 {
342 for (auto *existing_label :
343 labels) {
344 if (!existing_label
345 ->is_dominated &&
346 is_dominated<D, S>(
347 new_label,
348 existing_label)) {
349 ++stat_n_dom;
350 return true;
351 }
352 }
353 return false;
354 }
355 };
356 dominated = mother_bucket.check_dominance(
357 new_label, check_dominance_in_bucket,
358 stat_n_dom);
359 }
360
361 if (!dominated) {
362 // Recursively mark labels dominated by
363 // new_label.
364 auto set_dominated_recursive =
365 [](auto &self, Label *lbl) -> void {
366 lbl->set_dominated(true);
367 for (auto *child : lbl->children)
368 self(self, child);
369 };
370
371 for (auto *existing_label : to_bucket_labels) {
372 if (existing_label->is_dominated) continue;
373 if (is_dominated<D, S>(existing_label,
374 new_label)) {
375 existing_label->set_dominated(true);
376 set_dominated_recursive(
377 set_dominated_recursive,
378 existing_label);
379 }
380 }
381 ++n_labels;
382#ifdef SORTED_LABELS
383 mother_bucket.add_sorted_label(new_label);
384#elif defined(LIMITED_BUCKETS)
385 mother_bucket.sorted_label(new_label,
386 BUCKET_CAPACITY);
387#else
388 mother_bucket.add_label(new_label);
389#endif
390 // A new extension was successfully added.
391 all_ext = false;
392 }
393 } // End for each extended label.
394 } // End for each arc.
395
396 // Mark the label as extended (processed).
397 label->set_extended(true);
398 } // End for each label in bucket.
399 } // End for each bucket in current SCC.
400
401 // Update c_bar values for all buckets in the current SCC.
402 for (const int bucket : sorted_sccs[scc_index]) {
403 double min_c_bar = buckets[bucket].get_cb();
404 for (const auto phi_bucket : Phi[bucket]) {
405 min_c_bar = std::min(min_c_bar, c_bar[phi_bucket]);
406 }
407 c_bar[bucket] = min_c_bar;
408 }
409 } while (!all_ext);
410
411 } // End for each SCC (in topological order).
412
413 // Store the best label found from the labeling process.
414 if (Label *best_label_run =
415 get_best_label<D>(topological_order, c_bar, sccs)) {
416 best_label = best_label_run;
417 }
418
419 // Return the final c_bar vector.
420 return c_bar;
421}
Stage::One

◆ mono_initialization()

void BucketGraph::mono_initialization ( )

Initializes the BucketGraph for the mono-directional case.

Definition at line 598 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

598 {
599 // Clear previous data
600 merged_labels.clear();
601 merged_labels.reserve(100);
602 fw_c_bar.clear();
603 bw_c_bar.clear();
604
605 dominance_checks_per_bucket.assign(fw_buckets_size + 1, 0);
606 non_dominated_labels_per_bucket = 0;
607
608 // Resize cost vectors to match the number of buckets
609 fw_c_bar.resize(fw_buckets_size, std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
610 bw_c_bar.resize(bw_buckets_size, std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity());
611
612 auto &num_buckets =
613 assign_buckets<Direction::Forward>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
614 auto &num_bucket_index = assign_buckets<Direction::Forward>(
615 num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
616
617 int num_intervals = options.main_resources.size();
618 std::vector<double> total_ranges(num_intervals);
619 std::vector<double> base_intervals(num_intervals);
620
621 auto &VRPNode = nodes[0]; // Example for the first node
622
623 // Calculate base intervals and total ranges for each resource dimension
624 for (int r = 0; r < intervals.size(); ++r) {
625 base_intervals[r] =
626 (VRPNode.ub[r] - VRPNode.lb[r]) / intervals[r].interval;
627 }
628
629 // Clear forward and backward buckets
630 for (auto b = 0; b < fw_buckets.size(); b++) {
631 fw_buckets[b].clear();
632 bw_buckets[b].clear();
633 }
634
635 // Initialize forward buckets (generic for multiple dimensions)
636 std::vector<int> current_pos(num_intervals, 0);
637
638 std::vector<double> interval_starts(num_intervals);
639 std::vector<double> interval_ends(num_intervals);
640
641 // Initialize interval_starts to lower bounds and interval_ends to upper
642 // bounds
643 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
644 interval_starts[r] = VRPNode.lb[r];
645 interval_ends[r] = VRPNode.ub[r];
646 }
647 int offset_fw = 0;
648 int offset_bw = 0;
649
650 // Lambda to handle forward and backward direction initialization across
651 // combinations
652 auto initialize_intervals_combinations = [&](bool is_forward) {
653 int &offset = is_forward ? offset_fw : offset_bw;
654 auto &label_pool = is_forward ? label_pool_fw : label_pool_bw;
655 auto &buckets = is_forward ? fw_buckets : bw_buckets;
656 const auto &depot_id = is_forward ? options.depot : options.end_depot;
657 // print depot_id
658 int calculated_index_base =
659 is_forward ? num_buckets_index_fw[options.depot]
660 : num_buckets_index_bw[options.end_depot];
661
662 std::vector<int> current_pos(
663 num_intervals,
664 0); // Tracks current position in each interval dimension
665 std::vector<double> interval_bounds(num_intervals);
666
667 // Recursive lambda to generate all combinations of intervals
668 std::function<void(int)> generate_combinations = [&](int depth) {
669 if (depth == num_intervals) {
670 // All dimensions processed, now initialize for the current
671 // combination
672 auto depot = label_pool->acquire();
673 int calculated_index = calculated_index_base + offset;
674
675 // Set interval_bounds to current combination of interval
676 // starts or ends
677 for (int r = 0; r < num_intervals; ++r) {
678 interval_bounds[r] =
679 is_forward
680 ? interval_starts[r] +
681 current_pos[r] *
682 roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(base_intervals[r])
683 : interval_ends[r] -
684 current_pos[r] * roundToTwoDecimalPlaces(
685 base_intervals[r]);
686 }
687
688 // print interval
689 // Initialize depot with the current interval boundaries
690 depot->initialize(calculated_index, 0.0, interval_bounds,
691 depot_id);
692 depot->is_extended = false;
693 depot->cost += pstep_duals.getThreeTwoDualValue(depot_id);
694 depot->cost += pstep_duals.getThreeThreeDualValue(depot_id);
695 set_node_visited(depot->visited_bitmap, depot_id);
696 SRC_MODE_BLOCK(
697 depot->SRCmap.assign(cut_storage->SRCDuals.size(), 0);)
698 buckets[calculated_index].add_label(depot);
699 buckets[calculated_index].node_id = depot_id;
700
701 offset++; // Increment offset for each combination
702 return;
703 }
704
705 // Loop through each interval in the current dimension
706 for (int k = 0; k < intervals[depth].interval; ++k) {
707 current_pos[depth] = k;
708 generate_combinations(depth + 1);
709 }
710 };
711
712 // Start generating combinations from depth 0
713 generate_combinations(0);
714 };
715
716 // Call the lambda for both forward and backward directions, ensuring
717 // all combinations are processed
718 initialize_intervals_combinations(true); // Forward direction
719}
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ ObtainJumpBucketArcs()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::ObtainJumpBucketArcs ( )

Obtains jump bucket arcs for the BucketGraph.

This function iterates over each bucket in the BucketGraph and adds jump arcs to the set of buckets. It checks if each arc exists in the given Gamma vector for the current bucket. If an arc exists, it adds the corresponding bucket to the set of buckets to add jump arcs to. It then removes the non-component-wise minimal buckets from the set. Finally, it adds jump arcs from the current bucket to each bucket in the set.

Definition at line 338 of file BucketJump.h.

338 {
339 // Select direction-specific containers.
340 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
341 auto &fixed_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_fixed_buckets, bw_fixed_buckets);
342 auto &num_buckets_idx =
343 assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_index_fw, num_buckets_index_bw);
344 auto &num_buckets = assign_buckets<D>(num_buckets_fw, num_buckets_bw);
345 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
346 auto &buckets_size = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets_size, bw_buckets_size);
347
348 int arc_counter = 0; // Count of jump arcs added
349 int missing_counter = 0; // Count of missing paths
350
351 // Clear jump arcs for all nodes.
352 for (auto &node : nodes) {
353 node.clear_jump_arcs<D>();
354 }
355
356 // Process each bucket.
357 for (int b = 0; b < buckets_size; ++b) {
358 std::vector<int>
359 B_bar; // Temporary storage for valid adjacent bucket indices
360
361 const int current_node_id = buckets[b].node_id;
362 const auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
363 const auto &orig_arcs = nodes[current_node_id].template get_arcs<D>();
364
365 // Skip if there are no bucket arcs.
366 if (bucket_arcs.empty()) {
367 continue;
368 }
369
370 // For each original arc from the current node...
371 for (const auto &orig_arc : orig_arcs) {
372 const int from_node = orig_arc.from;
373 const int to_node = orig_arc.to;
374 bool have_path = false;
375
376 // Check if the current bucket arcs contain the desired path.
377 for (const auto &gamma : bucket_arcs) {
378 if (is_bucket_fixed<D>(gamma.from_bucket, gamma.to_bucket)) {
379 continue;
380 }
381
382 const int from_node_b = buckets[gamma.from_bucket].node_id;
383 const int to_node_b = buckets[gamma.to_bucket].node_id;
384
385 if (from_node == from_node_b && to_node == to_node_b) {
386 have_path = true;
387 break; // Path found: no need to search further.
388 }
389 }
390
391 // If no valid path was found, try to obtain a jump arc.
392 if (!have_path) {
393 ++missing_counter;
394
395 // Determine bucket range for the current node.
396 const int start_bucket = num_buckets_idx[current_node_id];
397 const int node_buckets = num_buckets[current_node_id];
398
399 // Lambda to process candidate adjacent buckets.
400 auto process_bucket = [&](int current_b, int candidate_b) {
401 // Only proceed if candidate bucket is adjacent (per Phi).
402 const auto &phi_candidate = Phi[candidate_b];
403 if (std::find(phi_candidate.begin(), phi_candidate.end(),
404 current_b) == phi_candidate.end())
405 return;
406
407 // Check candidate bucket's arcs.
408 const auto &candidate_arcs =
409 buckets[candidate_b].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
410 for (const auto &gamma_candidate : candidate_arcs) {
411 if (is_bucket_fixed<D>(gamma_candidate.from_bucket,
412 gamma_candidate.to_bucket))
413 continue;
414
415 const int from_node_candidate =
416 buckets[gamma_candidate.from_bucket].node_id;
417 const int to_node_candidate =
418 buckets[gamma_candidate.to_bucket].node_id;
419
420 // If the candidate arc matches the original arc...
421 if (from_node == from_node_candidate &&
422 to_node == to_node_candidate) {
423 B_bar.push_back(candidate_b);
424 const std::vector<double> res =
425 gamma_candidate.resource_increment;
426 const double cost = gamma_candidate.cost_increment;
427
428 // Add jump arcs both in the bucket and at the node
429 // level.
430 buckets[current_b].template add_jump_arc<D>(
431 current_b, candidate_b, res, cost);
432 nodes[buckets[current_b].node_id]
433 .template add_jump_arc<D>(
434 current_b, candidate_b, res, cost, to_node);
435 ++arc_counter;
436 }
437 }
438 };
439
440 // Process candidate buckets. For now both directions use
441 // increasing index. (For backward direction, adjust the loop as
442 // needed.)
443 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
444 for (int candidate_b = b + 1;
445 candidate_b < start_bucket + node_buckets;
446 ++candidate_b) {
447 process_bucket(b, candidate_b);
448 }
449 } else {
450 // Uncomment and adjust if backward should iterate in
451 // reverse order. for (int candidate_b = b - 1; candidate_b
452 // >= start_bucket; --candidate_b) {
453 // process_bucket(b, candidate_b);
454 // }
455 for (int candidate_b = b + 1;
456 candidate_b < start_bucket + node_buckets;
457 ++candidate_b) {
458 process_bucket(b, candidate_b);
459 }
460 }
461 }
462 }
463 }
464
465 // Print summary of jump arcs added.
466 CONDITIONAL(D, print_info("[Fw] added {} jump arcs\n", arc_counter),
467 print_info("[Bw] added {} jump arcs\n", arc_counter));
468}
CONDITIONAL
#define CONDITIONAL(D, FW_ACTION, BW_ACTION)

Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ print_statistics()

void BucketGraph::print_statistics ( )

Prints the statistics of the bucket graph.

This function outputs a formatted table displaying various metrics related to the bucket graph. The table includes headers and values for forward and backward labels, as well as dominance checks. The output is color-coded for better readability:

  • Bold blue for metric names
  • Green for values (backward labels)
  • Reset color to default after each line

The table structure: +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+ | Metric | Forward | Backward | +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+ | Labels | <stat_n_labels_fw> | <stat_n_labels_bw> | | Dominance Check | <stat_n_dom_fw> | <stat_n_dom_bw> | +-------------------—+--------------—+--------------—+

Note: The actual values for the metrics (e.g., stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw, etc.) should be provided by the corresponding member variables or functions.

Definition at line 756 of file BucketGraph.cpp.

756 {
757 const char *blue_bold = "\033[1;34m"; // Bold blue for metrics
758 const char *green = "\033[32m"; // Green for values (backward labels)
759 const char *reset = "\033[0m"; // Reset color
760
761 // Print table header with horizontal line and separators
762 fmt::print(
763 "\n+--------------------------------------------------------+\n");
764 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold, " Metric", reset,
765 " Forward", " Backward");
766 fmt::print("+--------------------------------------------------------+\n");
767
768 // Print labels for forward and backward with bold blue metric
769 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold, " Labels", reset,
770 stat_n_labels_fw, stat_n_labels_bw);
771
772 // Print dominated forward and backward labels with bold blue metric
773 fmt::print("|{}{:<20}{}| {:<15} | {:<15} |\n", blue_bold,
774 " Dominance Check", reset, stat_n_dom_fw, stat_n_dom_bw);
775
776 // Print the final horizontal line
777 fmt::print("+--------------------------------------------------------+\n");
778
779 fmt::print("\n");
780}

◆ process_all_resources()

template<Direction D, typename Gamma, typename VRPNode>
bool BucketGraph::process_all_resources ( std::vector< double > & new_resources,
const std::array< double, R_SIZE > & initial_resources,
const Gamma & gamma,
const VRPNode & theNode,
size_t N )

Processes all resources by iterating through them and applying constraints.

This function recursively processes each resource in the new_resources vector by calling process_resource for each index from I to N-1. If any resource processing fails (i.e., process_resource returns false), the function returns false immediately. If all resources are processed successfully, the function returns true.

Definition at line 114 of file BucketRes.h.

117 {
118 for (size_t I = 0; I < N; ++I) {
119 switch (static_cast<ResourceType>(options.resource_type[I])) {
120 [[likely]] case ResourceType::Disposable:
121 if (!process_disposable_resource<D>(
122 new_resources[I], initial_resources[I],
123 gamma.resource_increment[I], theNode.lb[I],
124 theNode.ub[I])) {
125 return false;
126 }
127 break;
128
129 case ResourceType::NonDisposable:
130 if (!process_non_disposable_resource<D>(
131 new_resources[I], initial_resources[I],
132 gamma.resource_increment[I], theNode.lb[I],
133 theNode.ub[I])) {
134 return false;
135 }
136 break;
137
138 case ResourceType::Binary:
139 new_resources[I] =
140 process_binary_resource<D>(gamma.resource_increment[I]);
141 break;
142
143 case ResourceType::Battery:
144 if (!process_battery_resource<D>(
145 new_resources[I], initial_resources[I],
146 theNode.is_station, options.battery_capacity)) {
147 return false;
148 }
149 break;
150
151 case ResourceType::RechargeCount:
152 if (!process_recharge_count<D>(
153 new_resources[I], initial_resources[I],
154 theNode.is_station, options.max_recharges)) {
155 return false;
156 }
157 break;
158
159 case ResourceType::RechargeTime:
160 if (!process_recharge_time<D>(
161 new_resources[I], initial_resources[I],
162 theNode.is_station, options.max_recharge_time)) {
163 return false;
164 }
165 break;
166
167 case ResourceType::MTW:
168 if (!process_mtw_resource<D>(
169 new_resources[I], initial_resources[I],
170 gamma.resource_increment[I], theNode)) {
171 return false;
172 }
173 break;
174 }
175 }
176 return true;
177}

◆ process_resource()

template<Direction D, typename Gamma, typename VRPNode>
bool BucketGraph::process_resource ( double & new_resource,
const std::array< double, R_SIZE > & initial_resources,
const Gamma & gamma,
const VRPNode & theNode,
size_t I )
constexpr

Processes a resource based on its disposability type and direction.

This function updates the new_resource value based on the initial resources, the increment provided by gamma, and the constraints defined by theNode. The behavior varies depending on the disposability type of the resource.

◆ redefine()

void BucketGraph::redefine ( int bucketInterval)
inline

Redefines the bucket intervals and reinitializes various data structures.

This function updates the bucket interval and reinitializes the intervals, buckets, fixed arcs, and fixed buckets. It also generates arcs and sorts them for each node.

◆ reset_fixed()

void BucketGraph::reset_fixed ( )
inline

Resets all fixed arcs in the graph.

This function iterates over each row in the fixed_arcs matrix and sets all elements to 0. It effectively clears any fixed arc constraints that may have been previously set.

◆ reset_pool()

void BucketGraph::reset_pool ( )
inline

Resets the forward and backward label pools.

This function resets both the forward (label_pool_fw) and backward (label_pool_bw) label pools to their initial states. It is typically used to clear any existing labels and prepare the pools for reuse.

◆ run_labeling_algorithms()

template<Stage state, Full fullness>
void BucketGraph::run_labeling_algorithms ( std::vector< double > & forward_cbar,
std::vector< double > & backward_cbar )

Runs forward and backward labeling algorithms in parallel and synchronizes the results.

T#his function creates tasks for forward and backward labeling algorithms using the provided scheduling mechanism. The tasks are executed in parallel, and the results are synchronized and stored in the provided vectors.

Definition at line 1034 of file BucketSolve.h.

1035 {
1036 // Schedule the forward labeling algorithm task on the scheduler.
1037 auto forward_task =
1038 stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() {
1039 return labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, state, fullness>();
1040 });
1041
1042 // Schedule the backward labeling algorithm task on the scheduler.
1043 auto backward_task =
1044 stdexec::schedule(bi_sched) | stdexec::then([&]() {
1045 return labeling_algorithm<Direction::Backward, state, fullness>();
1046 });
1047
1048 // Combine the forward and backward tasks.
1049 auto combined_work =
1050 stdexec::when_all(std::move(forward_task), std::move(backward_task)) |
1051 stdexec::then([&](auto forward_result, auto backward_result) {
1052 // Store the results into the provided vectors.
1053 forward_cbar = std::move(forward_result);
1054 backward_cbar = std::move(backward_result);
1055 });
1056
1057 // Wait for all tasks to complete.
1058 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(combined_work));
1059}
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ SCC_handler()

template<Direction D>
void BucketGraph::SCC_handler ( )

Handles the computation of Strongly Connected Components (SCCs) for the BucketGraph.

This function processes the bucket graph to identify SCCs using Tarjan's algorithm. It extends the bucket graph with arcs defined by the Phi sets, computes the SCCs, and orders them topologically. It also sorts the buckets within each SCC based on their lower or upper bounds, depending on the direction. Finally, it splits the arcs for each SCC and removes duplicates.

Definition at line 831 of file BucketUtils.h.

831 {
832 // Get necessary references.
833 auto &Phi = assign_buckets<D>(Phi_fw, Phi_bw);
834 auto &buckets = assign_buckets<D>(fw_buckets, bw_buckets);
835 auto &bucket_graph = assign_buckets<D>(fw_bucket_graph, bw_bucket_graph);
836
837 // Build an extended bucket graph from bucket_graph using Phi.
838 ankerl::unordered_dense::map<int, std::vector<int>> extended_bucket_graph =
839 bucket_graph;
840 for (size_t i = 0; i < extended_bucket_graph.size(); ++i) {
841 const auto &phi_set = Phi[i];
842 if (phi_set.empty()) continue;
843 for (auto phi_bucket : phi_set) {
844 extended_bucket_graph[phi_bucket].push_back(i);
845 }
846 }
847
848 // Compute SCCs using Tarjan's algorithm.
849 SCC scc_finder;
850 scc_finder.convertFromUnorderedMap(extended_bucket_graph);
851 auto sccs = scc_finder.tarjanSCC();
852 auto topological_order = scc_finder.topologicalOrderOfSCCs(sccs);
853
854#ifdef VERBOSE
855 // Optional: Print SCCs.
856 constexpr auto blue = "\033[34m";
857 constexpr auto reset = "\033[0m";
858 fmt::print((D == Direction::Forward) ? "FW SCCs:\n" : "BW SCCs:\n");
859 for (auto scc : topological_order) {
860 fmt::print("{}({}) -> {}", blue, scc, reset);
861 for (auto bucket : sccs[scc]) {
862 fmt::print("{} ", bucket);
863 }
864 fmt::print("\n");
865 }
866 fmt::print("\n");
867#endif
868
869 // Order SCCs according to the topological order.
870 std::vector<std::vector<int>> ordered_sccs;
871 ordered_sccs.reserve(sccs.size());
872 for (int scc : topological_order) {
873 ordered_sccs.push_back(sccs[scc]);
874 }
875
876 // Sort each SCC: For Forward, sort by bucket lower bound; for Backward,
877 // sort by bucket upper bound.
878 auto sorted_sccs = sccs; // Make a copy
879 for (auto &scc : sorted_sccs) {
880 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
881 pdqsort(scc.begin(), scc.end(), [&buckets](int a, int b) {
882 return buckets[a].lb[0] < buckets[b].lb[0];
883 });
884 } else {
885 pdqsort(scc.begin(), scc.end(), [&buckets](int a, int b) {
886 return buckets[a].ub[0] > buckets[b].ub[0];
887 });
888 }
889 }
890
891 // Ensure each VRP node has the appropriate SCC arc container allocated.
892 for (auto &node : nodes) {
893 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
894 node.fw_arcs_scc.resize(sccs.size());
895 } else {
896 node.bw_arcs_scc.resize(sccs.size());
897 }
898 }
899
900 // Lambda: Filter and collect arcs for a given bucket, node, and
901 // direction.
902 auto process_bucket_arcs = [&](int bucket, int scc_index) {
903 int from_node_id = buckets[bucket].node_id;
904 VRPNode &node = nodes[from_node_id];
905 // Select the proper arc container and node arcs based on the
906 // direction.
907 const auto &node_arcs =
908 (D == Direction::Forward) ? node.fw_arcs : node.bw_arcs;
909 std::vector<Arc> &filtered_arcs = (D == Direction::Forward)
910 ? node.fw_arcs_scc[scc_index]
911 : node.bw_arcs_scc[scc_index];
912
913 const auto &bucket_arcs = buckets[bucket].template get_bucket_arcs<D>();
914 for (const auto &arc : bucket_arcs) {
915 int to_node_id = buckets[arc.to_bucket].node_id;
916 // Look for an arc with destination equal to to_node_id.
917 auto it = std::find_if(
918 node_arcs.begin(), node_arcs.end(),
919 [&to_node_id](const Arc &a) { return a.to == to_node_id; });
920 if (it != node_arcs.end()) {
921 filtered_arcs.push_back(*it);
922 }
923 }
924 };
925
926 // For each SCC, process arcs for each bucket in the SCC.
927 int scc_ctr = 0;
928 for (const auto &scc : sccs) {
929 for (int bucket : scc) {
930 process_bucket_arcs(bucket, scc_ctr);
931 }
932 ++scc_ctr;
933 }
934
935 // Lambda: Sort and deduplicate arcs within each node's SCC arc
936 // container.
937 auto sortAndDedup = [](std::vector<Arc> &arcs) {
938 pdqsort(arcs.begin(), arcs.end(), [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) {
939 return std::tie(a.from, a.to) < std::tie(b.from, b.to);
940 });
941 auto last = std::unique(arcs.begin(), arcs.end(),
942 [](const Arc &a, const Arc &b) {
943 return a.from == b.from && a.to == b.to;
944 });
945 arcs.erase(last, arcs.end());
946 };
947
948 // For each node, sort and deduplicate all arc lists.
949 for (auto &node : nodes) {
950 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
951 for (auto &arcs : node.fw_arcs_scc) {
952 sortAndDedup(arcs);
953 }
954 } else {
955 for (auto &arcs : node.bw_arcs_scc) {
956 sortAndDedup(arcs);
957 }
958 }
959 }
960
961 // Build union-find structure for SCCs using the ordered SCCs.
962 UnionFind uf(ordered_sccs);
963 if constexpr (D == Direction::Forward) {
964 fw_ordered_sccs = ordered_sccs;
965 fw_topological_order = topological_order;
966 fw_sccs = sccs;
967 fw_sccs_sorted = sorted_sccs;
968 fw_union_find = uf;
969 } else {
970 bw_ordered_sccs = ordered_sccs;
971 bw_topological_order = topological_order;
972 bw_sccs = sccs;
973 bw_sccs_sorted = sorted_sccs;
974 bw_union_find = uf;
975 }
976}
blue
constexpr const char * blue
Definition Definitions.h:131

◆ set_adjacency_list()

template<Symmetry SYM>
void BucketGraph::set_adjacency_list ( )

Definition at line 1240 of file BucketUtils.h.

1240 {
1241 // === Step 1: Clear Existing Arcs from Each Node ===
1242 for (auto &node : nodes) {
1243 node.clear_arcs();
1244 }
1245
1246 // === Step 2: MST-Based Clustering ===
1247 MST mst_solver(nodes,
1248 [this](int from, int to) { return getcij(from, to); });
1249 auto mst = mst_solver.compute_mst();
1250
1251 // Collect and sort MST edge weights.
1252 std::vector<double> edge_weights;
1253 edge_weights.reserve(mst.size());
1254 for (const auto &[weight, from, to] : mst) {
1255 edge_weights.push_back(weight);
1256 }
1257 pdqsort(edge_weights.begin(), edge_weights.end());
1258
1259 // Compute theta as the 90th percentile (scaled down by 100).
1260 size_t p90_index = static_cast<size_t>(0.9 * edge_weights.size());
1261 double theta = (edge_weights[p90_index]) / 100.0;
1262 print_info("Computed theta: {}\n", theta);
1263
1264 // Cluster nodes and build mapping: job_id -> cluster_id.
1265 auto clusters = mst_solver.cluster(theta);
1266 print_info("Number of clusters: {}\n", clusters.size());
1267 std::vector<int> job_to_cluster(nodes.size(), -1);
1268 for (int cluster_id = 0; cluster_id < clusters.size(); ++cluster_id) {
1269 for (int job : clusters[cluster_id]) {
1270 job_to_cluster[job] = cluster_id;
1271 }
1272 }
1273
1274 // === Step 3: Precompute Resource Information ===
1275 // Find the index for the "time" resource.
1276 auto time_it =
1277 std::find(options.resources.begin(), options.resources.end(), "time");
1278 const int time_resource_idx =
1279 (time_it != options.resources.end())
1280 ? std::distance(options.resources.begin(), time_it)
1281 : -1;
1282
1283 // === Step 4: Process Arcs for Each Node ===
1284
1285 // Helper lambda to compute resource increments.
1286 // Depending on symmetry, consumption is either taken as-is or averaged.
1287 auto compute_resource_increments = [&](const VRPNode &from_node,
1288 const VRPNode &to_node,
1289 double travel_cost,
1290 std::vector<double> &inc) {
1291 const int nres = options.resources.size();
1292 if constexpr (SYM == Symmetry::Asymmetric) {
1293 for (int r = 0; r < nres; ++r) {
1294 inc[r] = (r == time_resource_idx)
1295 ? travel_cost + from_node.duration
1296 : from_node.consumption[r];
1297 }
1298 } else {
1299 for (int r = 0; r < nres; ++r) {
1300 inc[r] =
1301 (r == time_resource_idx)
1302 ? (from_node.duration / 2.0 + travel_cost +
1303 to_node.duration / 2.0)
1304 : ((from_node.consumption[r] + to_node.consumption[r]) /
1305 2.0);
1306 }
1307 }
1308 };
1309
1310 // Lambda to add forward and reverse arcs from a given node.
1311 auto add_arcs_for_node = [&](const VRPNode &curr_node) {
1312 // Containers to accumulate arcs before insertion.
1313 using ArcTuple = std::tuple<double, int, std::vector<double>, double>;
1314 std::vector<ArcTuple> forward_arcs;
1315 std::vector<ArcTuple> reverse_arcs;
1316 forward_arcs.reserve(nodes.size());
1317 reverse_arcs.reserve(nodes.size());
1318
1319 // Iterate over candidate next nodes.
1320 for (const auto &next_node : nodes) {
1321 // Skip if the candidate is a depot, the same node, or a pstep
1322 // depot.
1323 if (next_node.id == options.depot || curr_node.id == next_node.id ||
1324 (options.pstep && (next_node.id == options.pstep_depot ||
1325 next_node.id == options.pstep_end_depot))) {
1326 continue;
1327 }
1328
1329 double travel_cost = getcij(curr_node.id, next_node.id);
1330 double cost_inc = travel_cost - next_node.cost;
1331
1332 // Skip self-loops (or if bucket indexes match; here using node id
1333 // as bucket id).
1334 if (curr_node.id == next_node.id) continue;
1335
1336 // Compute resource increments.
1337 std::vector<double> res_inc(options.resources.size());
1338 compute_resource_increments(curr_node, next_node, travel_cost,
1339 res_inc);
1340
1341 // Check feasibility: ensure resource consumption does not exceed
1342 // next node's upper bound.
1343 bool feasible = true;
1344 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
1345 if (curr_node.lb[r] + res_inc[r] > next_node.ub[r]) {
1346 feasible = false;
1347 break;
1348 }
1349 }
1350 if (!feasible) continue;
1351
1352 // Compute priorities based on cluster membership.
1353 bool same_cluster =
1354 (job_to_cluster[curr_node.id] == job_to_cluster[next_node.id]);
1355 double forward_priority =
1356 (same_cluster ? 5.0 : 1.0) + 1.E-5 * next_node.start_time;
1357 double reverse_priority =
1358 (same_cluster ? 1.0 : 5.0) + 1.E-5 * curr_node.start_time;
1359
1360 // Collect arcs for forward and reverse directions.
1361 forward_arcs.emplace_back(forward_priority, next_node.id, res_inc,
1362 cost_inc);
1363 reverse_arcs.emplace_back(reverse_priority, next_node.id, res_inc,
1364 cost_inc);
1365 }
1366
1367 // Insert the arcs into the corresponding nodes.
1368 for (const auto &[priority, to_node, res_inc, cost_inc] :
1369 forward_arcs) {
1370 nodes[curr_node.id].template add_arc<Direction::Forward>(
1371 curr_node.id, to_node, res_inc, cost_inc, priority);
1372 }
1373 for (const auto &[priority, from_node, res_inc, cost_inc] :
1374 reverse_arcs) {
1375 nodes[from_node].template add_arc<Direction::Backward>(
1376 from_node, curr_node.id, res_inc, cost_inc, priority);
1377 }
1378 };
1379
1380 // Process each node, skipping the end depot.
1381 for (const auto &node : nodes) {
1382 if (node.id != options.end_depot) {
1383 add_arcs_for_node(node);
1384 }
1385 }
1386}

461 {
462 // Clear existing arcs for each node
463 for (auto &node : nodes) {
464 node.clear_arcs(); // Remove any existing arcs associated with the

466 }
467
468 // Step 1: Compute the clusters using MST-based clustering
469 MST mst_solver(nodes,
470 [&](int from, int to) { return this->getcij(from, to); });
471 double theta = 1.0; // Experiment with different values of θ
472 auto clusters = mst_solver.cluster(theta);
473
474 // Create a job-to-cluster mapping (cluster ID for each job/node)
475 std::vector<int> job_to_cluster(
476 nodes.size(), -1); // Mapping from job (node) to cluster ID
477 for (int cluster_id = 0; cluster_id < clusters.size(); ++cluster_id) {
478 for (int job : clusters[cluster_id]) {
479 job_to_cluster[job] = cluster_id;
480 }
481 }
482
483 // Step 2: Modify add_arcs_for_node to give priority based on cluster
484 // membership
485 auto add_arcs_for_node = [&](const VRPNode &node, int from_bucket,
486 std::vector<double> &res_inc) {
487 using Arc = std::tuple<double, int, std::vector<double>,
488 double>; // Arc: priority, to_node, resource
489 // increments, cost increment
490
491 std::vector<Arc> best_arcs;
492 best_arcs.reserve(nodes.size()); // Reserve space for forward arcs
493
494 std::vector<Arc> best_arcs_rev;
495 best_arcs_rev.reserve(nodes.size()); // Reserve space for reverse arcs
496
497 for (const auto &next_node : nodes) {
498 if (!manual_arcs.has_arc(node.id, next_node.id))
499 continue; // Skip arcs not in the manual list
500
501 if (next_node.id == options.depot || node.id == next_node.id)
502 continue; // Skip depot and same node
503
504 auto travel_cost =
505 getcij(node.id, next_node.id); // Calculate travel cost
506 double cost_inc =
507 travel_cost - next_node.cost; // Adjust cost increment by
508 // subtracting next node's cost
509
510 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
511 if (options.resources[r] == "time") {
512 res_inc[r] = travel_cost +
513 node.duration; // Update resource increment
514 // based on node duration
515 } else {
516 res_inc[r] = node.consumption[r];
517 }
518 }
519 // res_inc[TIME_INDEX] += travel_cost; // Add travel time to
520 // resource increment
521
522 int to_bucket = next_node.id;
523 if (from_bucket == to_bucket)
524 continue; // Skip arcs that loop back to the same bucket
525
526 bool feasible =
527 true; // Check feasibility based on resource constraints
528 for (int r = 0; r < options.resources.size(); ++r) {
529 if (node.lb[r] + res_inc[r] > next_node.ub[r]) {
530 feasible = false;
531 break;
532 }
533 }
534 if (!feasible) continue; // Skip infeasible arcs
535
536 // Step 3: Calculate priority based on cluster membership
537 double priority_value;
538 double reverse_priority_value;
539 if (job_to_cluster[node.id] == job_to_cluster[next_node.id]) {
540 // Higher priority if both nodes are in the same cluster
541 priority_value =
542 5.0 +
543 1.E-5 * next_node.start_time; // Adjust weight for
544 // same-cluster priority
545 reverse_priority_value =
546 1.0 + 1.E-5 * node.start_time; // Adjust weight for
547 // same-cluster priority
548 } else {
549 // Lower priority for cross-cluster arcs
550 priority_value =
551 1.0 +
552 1.E-5 * next_node.start_time; // Higher base value for
553 // cross-cluster arcs
554 reverse_priority_value =
555 5.0 + 1.E-5 * node.start_time; // Higher base value for
556 // cross-cluster arcs
557 }
558
559 best_arcs.emplace_back(priority_value, next_node.id, res_inc,
560 cost_inc); // Store the forward arc
561 best_arcs_rev.emplace_back(reverse_priority_value, next_node.id,
562 res_inc,
563 cost_inc); // Store the reverse arc
564 }
565
566 // Add forward arcs from the current node to its neighbors
567 for (const auto &arc : best_arcs) {
568 auto [priority_value, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc] = arc;
569 nodes[node.id].template add_arc<Direction::Forward>(
570 node.id, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc,
571 priority_value); // Add forward arc
572 }
573
574 // Add reverse arcs from neighboring nodes to the current node
575 for (const auto &arc : best_arcs_rev) {
576 auto [priority_value, to_bucket, res_inc_local, cost_inc] = arc;
577 nodes[to_bucket].template add_arc<Direction::Backward>(
578 to_bucket, node.id, res_inc_local, cost_inc,
579 priority_value); // Add reverse arc
580 }
581 };
582
583 // Step 4: Iterate over all nodes to set the adjacency list
584 for (const auto &VRPNode : nodes) {
585 if (VRPNode.id == options.end_depot)
586 continue; // Skip the last node (depot)
587
588 std::vector<double> res_inc(
589 intervals.size()); // Resource increment vector
590 add_arcs_for_node(VRPNode, VRPNode.id,
591 res_inc); // Add arcs for the current node
592 }
593}

void BucketGraph::set_distance_matrix ( const std::vector< std::vector< double > > & distanceMatrix,

Sets the distance matrix and calculates neighborhoods.

This function assigns the provided distance matrix to the internal distance matrix of the class and then calculates the neighborhoods based on the given number of nearest neighbors.

static void BucketGraph::set_node_unreachable ( std::array< uint64_t, num_words > & bitmap,
int node_id )
inlinestatic

Marks a node as unreachable in the given bitmap.

This function sets the bit corresponding to the specified node_id in the bitmap, indicating that the node is unreachable.

612 node_id & 63; // Determine the bit position within that segment
613 bitmap[word_index] |= (1ULL << bit_position);
614 }

void BucketGraph::setDuals ( const std::vector< double > & duals)
inline

Sets the dual values for the nodes.

This function assigns the provided dual values to the nodes. It iterates through the given vector of duals and sets each node's dual value to the corresponding value from the vector.

◆ shallUpdateStep()

bool BucketGraph::shallUpdateStep ( )
inline

Definition at line 1329 of file BucketGraph.h.

1329 {
1330 int update_ctr = 0;
1331 ankerl::unordered_dense::set<int> updated_buckets;
1332
1333 // Iterate over all buckets and check if either forward or backward
1334 // bucket indicates a split is needed.
1335 for (size_t b = 0; b < fw_buckets.size(); ++b) {
1336 if (fw_buckets[b].shall_split || bw_buckets[b].shall_split) {
1337 const int inner_id = fw_buckets[b].node_id;
1338 // Skip depot buckets.
1339 if (inner_id == options.depot ||
1340 inner_id == options.end_depot) {
1341 continue;
1342 }
1343 // If this node was already updated, skip it.
1344 if (updated_buckets.contains(inner_id)) {
1345 continue;
1346 }
1347 // If the bucket split value is already at maximum (100), skip.
1348 if (fw_bucket_splits[inner_id] == 100) {
1349 continue;
1350 }
1351 // Update the bucket splits by doubling the current value,
1352 // capped at 100.
1353 const int update_target =
1354 std::min(fw_bucket_splits[inner_id] * 2, 100);
1355 fw_bucket_splits[inner_id] = update_target;
1356 bw_bucket_splits[inner_id] = update_target;
1357 updated_buckets.insert(inner_id);
1358 update_ctr++;
1359 }
1360 }
1361
1362 // If we have reached a significant number of updates and we haven't
1363 // accumulated too many cuts, then update bucket splits.
1364 const int CHANGE_THRESHOLD = nodes.size() / 6;
1365 if (update_ctr >= CHANGE_THRESHOLD && cut_storage->size() < 40) {
1366 // Clear union caches.
1367 fw_union_cache.clear();
1368 bw_union_cache.clear();
1369 print_info("Updating bucket splits with {} changes\n", update_ctr);
1370
1371 // Redefine buckets in parallel for both forward and backward
1372 // directions.
1373 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
1374 work, bi_sched,
1375 SECTION {
1376 // Forward bucket redefinition.
1377 define_buckets<Direction::Forward>();
1378 },
1379 SECTION {
1380 // Backward bucket redefinition.
1381 define_buckets<Direction::Backward>();
1382 });
1383
1384 // Regenerate arcs after updating bucket splits.
1385 generate_arcs();
1386
1387 // Process jump bucket arcs in parallel.
1388 PARALLEL_SECTIONS(
1389 workB, bi_sched,
1390 SECTION { ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Forward>(); },
1391 SECTION { ObtainJumpBucketArcs<Direction::Backward>(); });
1392
1393 return true;
1394 }
1395 return false;
1396 }

◆ solve()

template<Symmetry SYM>
std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::solve ( bool trigger = false)
inline

Solves the bucket graph optimization problem using a multi-stage bi-labeling algorithm.

The function iteratively adjusts the terminal time and handles different stages of the bi-labeling algorithm to find the optimal paths. The stages are adaptive and transition based on the inner objective values and iteration counts.

Definition at line 55 of file BucketSolve.h.

55 {
56 // Start with a nonoptimal status.
57 status = Status::NotOptimal;
58 if (trigger) {
59 transition = true;
60 fixed = false;
61 }
62
63 updateSplit(); // Update split values for the bucket graph.
64 std::vector<Label *> paths;
65
67 // Adaptive Stage Handling
69
70 // ----- Stage 1: Light Heuristic -----
71 if (s1 && depth == 0) {
72 stage = 1;
73 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::One>();
74 // inner_obj = paths[0]->cost; // (if available)
75 if (inner_obj >= -1 || iter >= 10) {
76 s1 = false;
77 s2 = true; // Transition to Stage 2.
78 }
79 }
80 // ----- Stage 2: Expensive Pricing Heuristic -----
81 else if (s2 && depth == 0) {
82 stage = 2;
83 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::Two>();
84 // inner_obj = paths[0]->cost; // (if available)
85 if (inner_obj >= -10 || iter > 500) {
86 s2 = false;
87 s3 = true; // Transition directly to Stage 4.
88 }
89 }
90 // ----- Stage 3: Heuristic Fixing Approach -----
91 else if (s3 && depth == 0) {
92 stage = 3;
93 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::Three>();
94 // inner_obj = paths[0]->cost; // (if available)
95 if (inner_obj >= -0.5) {
96 s3 = false;
97 s4 = true;
98 transition = true; // Prepare for Stage 4 transition.
99 }
100 }
101 // ----- Stage 4: Exact Labeling with Fixing Enabled -----
102 else if (s4 && depth == 0) {
103 stage = 4;
104#ifdef FIX_BUCKETS
105 if (transition) {
106 // During a transition to Stage 4, fix buckets temporarily.
107 bool original_fixed = fixed;
108 fixed = true;
109 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::Four>();
110 transition = false;
111 fixed = original_fixed;
112 min_red_cost = paths[0]->cost; // Update minimum reduced cost.
113 iter++;
114 return paths;
115 }
116#endif
117 // Standard Stage 4 processing.
118 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::Four>();
119 // inner_obj = paths[0]->cost; // (if available)
120 bool rollback = false;
121 if (status != Status::Rollback) {
122 rollback = shallUpdateStep();
123 }
124 if (rollback) {
125 status = Status::Rollback;
126 return paths;
127 }
128 // Compute a new threshold for separation based on stats.
129 threshold = stats.computeThreshold(iter, inner_obj);
130 // If objective is sufficiently high, enter separation mode.
131 if (inner_obj > threshold) {
132 ss = true;
133#if !defined(SRC) && !defined(SRC3)
134 status = Status::Optimal;
135 return paths;
136#endif
137 status = Status::Separation;
138 }
139 }
140 // ----- Stage 5: Enumeration -----
141 else if (s5 && depth == 0) {
142 stage = 5;
143 print_info("Starting enumeration with gap {}\n", gap);
144 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::Enumerate>();
145 print_info("Finished enumeration with {} paths\n", paths.size());
146 status = Status::Optimal;
147 }
148
149 // Increment the iteration counter.
150 iter++;
151 return paths;
152}

std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::solveHeuristic ( )
inline

Definition at line 154 of file BucketSolve.h.

154 {
155 // Initialize the status as not optimal at the start
156 status = Status::NotOptimal;
157
158 updateSplit(); // Update the split values for the bucket graph
159
160 // Placeholder for the final paths (labels) and inner objective value
161 std::vector<Label *> paths;
162 double inner_obj;
163
165 // ADAPTIVE STAGE HANDLING
167 // Stage 1: Apply a light heuristic (Stage::One)
168 if (s1) {
169 stage = 1;
170 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::One>(); // Solve the problem with
171 // Stage 1 heuristic
172 inner_obj = paths[0]->cost;
173
174 // Transition from Stage 1 to Stage 2 if the objective improves or after
175 // 10 iterations
176 if (inner_obj >= -5 || iter >= 10) {
177 s1 = false;
178 s2 = true; // Move to Stage 2
179 }
180 }
181 // Stage 2: Apply a more expensive pricing heuristic (Stage::Two)
182 else if (s2) {
183 s2 = true;
184 stage = 2;
185 paths = bi_labeling_algorithm<Stage::Two>(); // Solve the problem with
186 // Stage 2 heuristic
187 inner_obj = paths[0]->cost;
188
189 // Transition from Stage 2 to Stage 3 if the objective improves or after
190 // 800 iterations
191 if (inner_obj >= -100 || iter > 800) {
192 status = Status::Optimal;
193 }
194 }
195
196 iter++; // Increment the iteration counter
197
198 return paths; // Return the final paths after processing
199}
Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ solvePSTEP()

std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::solvePSTEP ( PSTEPDuals & inner_pstep_duals)

Solves the PSTEP problem and returns a vector of labels representing paths.

This function performs the following steps:

  1. Resets the pool.
  2. Initializes the mono algorithm.
  3. Runs a labeling algorithm in the forward direction.
  4. Iterates through the forward buckets and computes new labels.
  5. Filters and collects labels that meet the criteria.
Returns
std::vector<Label*> A vector of pointers to Label objects representing the paths.

Definition at line 24 of file BucketPSTEP.cpp.

24 {
25
26 const int CHUNK_SIZE = 10;
27
28 std::vector<Label *> all_paths;
29 std::vector<double> all_costs;
30 std::vector<int> all_firsts, all_lasts;
31
32 // Mutex for shared resources
33 std::mutex cuts_mutex;
34
35 const int JOBS = nodes.size();
36 exec::static_thread_pool pool(std::thread::hardware_concurrency());
37 auto sched = pool.get_scheduler();
38
39 std::vector<std::pair<int, int>> tasks;
40 for (int i = 0; i < JOBS - 1; ++i) {
41 for (int j = 1; j < JOBS; ++j) {
42 if (i == j) continue;
43 tasks.emplace_back(i, j);
44 }
45 }
46 auto bulk_sender =
47 stdexec::bulk(stdexec::just(), (tasks.size() + CHUNK_SIZE - 1) / CHUNK_SIZE, // Calculate total chunks
48 [&](std::size_t chunk_idx) {
49 // Calculate indices i, j from chunk_idx
50 size_t start_idx = chunk_idx * CHUNK_SIZE;
51 size_t end_idx = std::min(start_idx + CHUNK_SIZE, tasks.size());
52 for (size_t task_idx = start_idx; task_idx < end_idx; ++task_idx) {
53 auto [i, j] = tasks[task_idx];
54 BucketOptions sub_options;
55 auto sub_bg = BucketGraph(nodes, R_max[0], 20);
56 sub_bg.set_distance_matrix(distance_matrix);
57 sub_options.depot = i;
58 sub_options.end_depot = j;
59 // print i and j
60 sub_options.pstep = true; // print depot and end_depot
61 sub_options.max_path_size = options.max_path_size;
62 sub_options.min_path_size = options.min_path_size;
63 sub_bg.setOptions(sub_options);
64 sub_bg.setPSTEPduals(inner_pstep_duals);
65 sub_bg.setup();
66 sub_bg.reset_pool();
67 sub_bg.mono_initialization();
68 // Solve and get the paths
69 std::vector<double> forward_cbar(sub_bg.fw_buckets.size());
70 forward_cbar = sub_bg.labeling_algorithm<Direction::Forward, Stage::Four, Full::PSTEP>();
71 std::vector<Label *> paths;
72 // print paths.size
73 for (auto bucket : std::ranges::iota_view(0, sub_bg.fw_buckets_size)) {
74 auto bucket_labels = sub_bg.fw_buckets[bucket].get_labels();
75 // print bucket_labels size
76 for (auto label : bucket_labels) {
77 if (!label) continue;
78 auto new_label = compute_mono_label(label);
79 if ((new_label->nodes_covered.size() > 1) &&
80 (new_label->nodes_covered.back() == sub_options.end_depot) &&
81 (new_label->nodes_covered.size() >= options.min_path_size &&
82 new_label->nodes_covered.size() <= options.max_path_size)) {
83 paths.push_back(new_label);
84 }
85 }
86 }
87
88 // Check if no paths are found
89 if (paths.empty()) return;
90
91 // Lock to protect shared resources
92 {
93 std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(cuts_mutex);
94 // Process the found paths
95 for (const auto &path : paths) { all_paths.push_back(path); }
96 }
97 // }
98 }
99 });
100
101 // Submit work to the thread pool and wait for completion
102 auto work = stdexec::starts_on(sched, bulk_sender);
103 stdexec::sync_wait(std::move(work));
104
105 return all_paths;
106}

std::vector< Label * > BucketGraph::solvePSTEP_by_MTZ ( )

Definition at line 356 of file BucketPSTEP.cpp.

356 {
357 int JOBS = nodes.size();
358
359 std::vector<std::vector<uint16_t>> all_paths;
360 std::vector<double> all_costs;
361 std::vector<int> all_firsts;
362 std::vector<int> all_lasts;
363
364 auto duals = solveTSP(all_paths, all_costs, all_firsts, all_lasts, distance_matrix, true);
365 auto max_pstep_iterations = 10;
366 for (auto z = 0; z < max_pstep_iterations; ++z) {
367 fmt::print("--------------------------------------\n");
368 fmt::print("Iteration {}\n", z);
369 auto three_two_duals = std::get<0>(duals);
370 auto three_three_duals = std::get<1>(duals);
371 auto three_five_duals = std::get<2>(duals);
372 PSTEPDuals inner_pstep_duals;
373
374 // Convert three_two to vector of pairs
375 std::vector<std::pair<int, double>> three_two_tuples;
376 three_two_tuples.reserve(three_two_duals.size());
377 std::transform(three_two_duals.begin(), three_two_duals.end(), std::back_inserter(three_two_tuples),
378 [index = 0](double value) mutable { return std::make_pair(index++, value); });
379 inner_pstep_duals.setThreeTwoDualValues(std::move(three_two_tuples));
380
381 // Convert three_three to vector of pairs
382 std::vector<std::pair<int, double>> three_three_tuples;
383 three_three_tuples.reserve(three_three_duals.size());
384 std::transform(three_three_duals.begin(), three_three_duals.end(), std::back_inserter(three_three_tuples),
385 [index = 0](double value) mutable { return std::make_pair(index++, value); });
386 inner_pstep_duals.setThreeThreeDualValues(std::move(three_three_tuples));
387
388 // Convert three_five to vector of pairs of pairs
389 std::vector<std::pair<std::pair<int, int>, double>> arc_duals_tuples;
390 size_t total_size = 0;
391 for (const auto &row : three_five_duals) total_size += row.size();
392 arc_duals_tuples.reserve(total_size);
393
394 for (size_t i = 0; i < three_five_duals.size(); ++i) {
395 for (size_t j = 0; j < three_five_duals[i].size(); ++j) {
396 if (i != j) { // Skip diagonal or add specific condition if needed
397 arc_duals_tuples.emplace_back(std::pair{i, j}, three_five_duals[i][j]);
398 }
399 }
400 }
401 inner_pstep_duals.setArcDualValues(std::move(arc_duals_tuples));
402
403 auto sub_paths = solvePSTEP(inner_pstep_duals);
404 // print sub_paths.size
405 auto paths_added = 0;
406 for (auto path : sub_paths) {
407 if (path->cost >= 0) { continue; }
408 paths_added++;
409 all_paths.push_back(path->getRoute());
410 all_costs.push_back(path->real_cost);
411 all_firsts.push_back(path->nodes_covered.front());
412 all_lasts.push_back(path->nodes_covered.back());
413 }
414 if (paths_added == 0) { break; }
415
416 duals = solveTSP(all_paths, all_costs, all_firsts, all_lasts, distance_matrix);
417 }
418 return {};
419}

auto BucketGraph::solveTSP ( std::vector< std::vector< uint16_t > > & paths,
std::vector< double > & path_costs,
std::vector< int > & firsts,

std::vector< std::vector< double > > & cost_matrix,
